Careers at MAA

The Municipal Assessment Agency is a human-centered organization. We are committed to creating and fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive work environment that reflects the people that live in Newfoundland and Labrador. We are committed to working together with the communities we serve to forge strong partnerships and build a strong team of dedicated professionals across the province.

Careers

There are currently no active job postings. We encourage you to visit our careers page regularly for other upcoming opportunities.

For further information about a career at the Office of the Auditor General, please contact our Manager of People and Culture, Andrea Milley at andreamilley@oag.nl.ca or call 709-725-3351.

The Office of the Auditor General values diversity in the workplace and is an equal-opportunity employer.

Disability-related accommodations and alternate formats related to competitions are available on request by contacting 709-725-3351.

Preference will be given to applicants who are Canadian citizens or permanent residents of Canada, or those who are legally entitled to work in Canada.

Competitions posted may be used to fill future similar vacancies with the Office of the Auditor General.

How to Apply:

Applications must be received on or before the closing date stated for a job posting. It is the responsibility of the applicant to submit an application that demonstrates the required merit criteria. All applications must contain accurate contact information, including current mailing address, email address and phone number. All information submitted as part of an application must be factual, complete and current to the date of submission. Applicants must submit by email to careers@oag.nl.ca

Applications should be consolidated into a single PDF document

Home > Join the RNC > Civilian Opportunities
Civilian Opportunities

The RNC has roughly 100 civilian employees who play an important role in the delivery of police services every day. Civilians are employed in a wide variety of disciplines including finance, administration, planning, communication, information management and technology, legal services, as well as asset and facilities management.

Civilian employment opportunities available within the RNC are advertised through the Public Service Commission, Government of Newfoundland and Labrador. Visit https://www.hiring.gov.nl.ca to view current job postings.

title: Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner (Two Year Term)
description: Venor is proud to partner with the Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC) on their search for a Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner to join their team in Halifax, Nova Scotia. The Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC) plays an important role in the province, responsibly managing the sale of beverage alcohol and cannabis while investing back into the communities it serves. Through innovation, operational excellence, and a commitment to public service, the NSLC continues to modernize its business and technology landscape to better serve Nova Scotians. As part of this transformation, NSLC is embarking on a significant SAP modernization initiative and is seeking a Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner (BPO) to help shape the future of enterprise reporting and analytics. This two-year term opportunity offers the chance to play a pivotal role in one of the organization's most strategic transformation programs, ensuring data, reporting, and business intelligence capabilities are designed to support informed decision-making across the enterprise. Reporting to the Director, Insights & Strategy, this role will serve as the primary business owner for reporting strategy, analytics alignment, and enterprise data definitions while partnering closely with business leaders, project teams, and technology stakeholders throughout the SAP implementation. The Opportunity This role sits at the intersection of business strategy, analytics, and enterprise transformation. Acting as the

title: Senior Structural Engineer
location: St John's, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Newfoundland & Labrador, Ontario, CA
description: Senior Structural Engineer  Venor is proud to partner with Tiller Engineering Inc. in their search for a Senior Structural Engineer.  At Tiller Engineering, you'll join a team that has built its reputation on delivering practical, innovative structural engineering solutions for over two decades. Since 2001, Tiller has partnered with clients across the telecommunications, infrastructure, and energy sectors, providing engineering expertise on projects that strengthen communities throughout Atlantic Canada.  As the company continues to grow, they are looking for an experienced Structural Engineer who is eager to take on technically challenging work, mentor others, and play an influential role in shaping projects from concept through completion.  The Opportunity  This is an opportunity to step into a senior technical role where your expertise will directly influence the success of a wide variety of projects. You'll lead structural analysis and design, provide technical oversight, collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, and build trusted relationships with clients and project partners.  You'll enjoy the autonomy to make sound engineering decisions while working alongside experienced professionals who value collaboration, practical thinking, and delivering quality work. Whether you're designing new infrastructure, assessing existing structures, or solving complex engineering challenges, you'll have the opportunity to make a meaningful impact every day.  What You'll Do  As a Senio

title: Dental Faculty Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with Keyin College in the search for a Dental Faculty Coordinator for its Level II Dental Assisting Program.  About Keyin College  Keyin College is one of Newfoundland and Labrador's leading post-secondary institutions, with more than 40,000 graduates since 1980. As the province's largest private college, Keyin delivers career-focused education that equips learners with the practical, in-demand skills needed to succeed in today's workforce.  The Level II Dental Assisting Program reflects this commitment through a nationally approved curriculum that combines classroom learning with hands-on clinical experience in fully equipped dental clinics. This immersive approach equips students with the knowledge, confidence, and real-world skills required to thrive in modern dental practices.  Guided by a collaborative, student-centred culture, Keyin College is committed to educational excellence while fostering the success of its students, faculty, and staff. Joining Keyin means becoming part of an institution dedicated to developing the next generation of skilled healthcare professionals.  The Role  Keyin College is looking for a Dental Faculty Coordinator to lead and support the Level II Dental Assisting Program. This role is responsible for overseeing program delivery, fostering instructional excellence, and ensuring the curriculum continues to meet professional, regulatory, and industry standards. Working closely with instructors, students, and campus leade

title: Senior Sales Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with InnovMarine on their search for a Senior Sales Engineer to join their growing team! InnovMarine delivers innovative software solutions and consulting services that help organizations optimize complex asset management operations across the maritime, defence, and industrial sectors. Combining deep technical expertise with industry knowledge, InnovMarine partners with organizations to modernize operations, improve decision-making, and unlock greater value through digital transformation and emerging technologies. As InnovMarine continues to expand across Atlantic Canada, they are seeking a Senior Sales Engineer to help grow their presence within the maritime and defence industries. This unique opportunity blends technical pre-sales expertise with consultative enterprise sales, making it ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex business challenges into practical technology solutions. Reporting directly to the President, you'll play a key role in expanding existing enterprise relationships while developing new business opportunities throughout Atlantic Canada. Working closely with technical and consulting teams, you'll help organizations leverage solutions such as QuickBrain and IBM Maximo to improve asset management, operational performance, and long-term business outcomes. This is a remote position open to candidates residing in Atlantic Canada, with regular travel throughout the region. The Opportunity This role sits at the intersection of

title: Accountant
description: Venor is proud to partner with Archibald Drilling & Blasting in their search for an Accountant to join their team in Truro, Nova Scotia. Archibald Drilling & Blasting is a leading provider of drilling and blasting services, with more than 40 years of experience delivering safe, innovative, and reliable solutions across quarry operations, open-pit mining, civil construction, and marine projects. Known for its commitment to safety, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, Archibald supports some of the most complex and demanding projects throughout Atlantic Canada and beyond. Reporting directly to General Manager, the Accountant will play a critical role in the day-to-day financial operations of the business. This is a highly visible and well-rounded position that will touch all aspects of accounting and finance, while helping leadership navigate cash flow management, operational reporting, and continuous process improvement. The successful candidate will thrive in a fast-paced environment, bring a proactive and solutions-focused mindset, and be motivated by the opportunity to help modernize and improve financial processes as the business continues to grow. What you’ll be doing Managing full-cycle accounting functions across the organization Overseeing accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll support, reconciliations, journal entries, and general ledger maintenance. Preparing monthly financial statements, reports, and cash flow projections to support operational

title: Director of Operations
description: Venor is proud to partner with Laura Gatien & Associates in their search for a Director of Operations to join their team. They are seeking a people-centric leader who can bring structure, clarity, and steady leadership while championing the values that make LGA unique. About Laura Gatien & Associates Laura Gatien & Associates is a growing counselling practice dedicated to providing accessible, client-focused mental health support within the community. Guided by compassion, professionalism, and a deep commitment to social impact, LGA supports individuals and families through evidence-based care and a welcoming environment. Their work is shaped by five core values:  Warmth, Dedication, Vulnerability, Equality, and Growth, which inform how they care for clients, collaborate as a team, and show up for their community. This role is a strong fit for an experienced operations leader who enjoys working alongside a dynamic entrepreneur to bring ideas to life and turn vision into execution. The Director of Operations will serve as the CEO’s essential complement, harmonizing the major functions of the business, fostering accountability, strengthening culture, and driving sustainable traction. What Success Looks Like This is not a role where you'll be expected to take the reins on day one. Instead, you'll benefit from a thoughtful, phased onboarding designed to set you up for long-term success. Working alongside the  Owner, you'll spend your first 6-12 months learning every aspect of the

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with QuickFacts on their search for a Senior Platform Engineer to join their growing team! QuickFacts is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) company transforming how insurance professionals access and manage information. Their platform brings together underwriting guidelines, carrier documentation, workflows, training resources, and comparison tools into a single, searchable experience that saves clients valuable time and improves decision-making. As QuickFacts continues to expand its AI-powered platform, they are seeking a seasoned backend engineer who thrives on building scalable systems, solving complex technical challenges, and developing innovative solutions that deliver meaningful business impact. This is an opportunity to join a growing technology company where your work will directly influence product strategy, platform architecture, and the future of AI-driven information management within the insurance industry. The Opportunity As a Senior Platform Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing and scaling the services, infrastructure, APIs, and data systems that power QuickFacts' next generation of products. This position sits at the intersection of software engineering, large-scale information systems, and applied AI. You'll work closely with Product, Front-End Engineering, and DevOps teams to transform complex insurance data into intelligent customer experiences. From designing scalable microservices and data pipelines to optimizing AI-

title: Senior Project Manager
description: Our client, Nexco Construction Company is growing and seeking an ambitious and results-driven Senior Project Manager based in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia. This is an opportunity for a proven construction leader who thrives on ownership, enjoys solving complex challenges, and wants to play a key role in delivering impactful civil infrastructure projects.  About the Role  As a Senior Project Manager, you will lead projects from pre-construction through closeout, overseeing all aspects of planning, execution, financial performance, and client management. Working closely with operations, field leadership, subcontractors, engineers, and clients, you will be empowered to drive project success while contributing to the continued growth and evolution of the organization. We are looking for a leader who is proactive, highly accountable, and motivated by delivering exceptional results in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.  Key Responsibilities  Lead the successful delivery of civil construction projects from pre-construction through completion and closeout.   Develop, manage, and maintain project schedules, budgets, forecasts, and resource plans.   Oversee project financial performance, identifying risks and opportunities to maximize profitability.   Coordinate and manage subcontractors, suppliers, consultants, and internal project teams.   Ensure projects are executed safely, efficiently, and in accordance with contract requirements and quality standards.   Build and maintain stron

title: Social Worker
description: Venor is proud to partner with Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services in their search for a Social Worker to join their team. Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services (OCFS) is a designated child and family services agency dedicated to serving the children and families of Oromocto First Nation. Grounded in community, culture, and collaboration, OCFS works to keep children safe, strengthen families, and ensure children remain connected to their identity, traditions, and support systems. Unlike larger agencies, OCFS serves a single community, allowing for smaller caseloads and a more personalized approach to care. Team members work closely together, creating an environment where social workers can devote meaningful time and attention to each child and family while receiving strong organizational support. Reporting to the Director of OCFS, the Social Worker will play a critical role in promoting the safety, wellbeing, and long-term success of children and families within the community. This is a full-time, on-site opportunity based in Oromocto First Nation, New Brunswick, with local travel required and participation in a rotating after-hours on-call schedule. What you'll be doing Conduct child protection investigations, risk assessments, and safety assessments to support the wellbeing of children and families. Develop and manage case plans focused on family preservation, reunification, and kinship or community-based care. Support families navigating complex chall

title: Branch Manager
description: Our client, Graybar is seeking a dynamic and results-driven Branch Manager to lead and grow the branch operations in Kentville, Nova Scotia. This role is ideal for a strategic leader who thrives on developing business opportunities, driving operational excellence, and building high-performing teams. Reporting to the Regional Vice President, the Branch Manager, you will have full accountability for branch performance—balancing sales growth, customer satisfaction, financial management, and employee development—while acting as a key ambassador of the company in the local market. What You’ll Do Leadership & Strategy Provide strategic direction and lead the execution of branch initiatives aligned with company objectives. Champion a culture of accountability, integrity, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Act as a role model by consistently following company policies, procedures, and values. Sales & Customer Growth Maintain and grow sales volume while strengthening customer relationships. Identify and develop new business opportunities, products, and customers. Build strong relationships with customers and suppliers through meetings, calls, and industry events. Ensure all reasonable customer requests are handled promptly and professionally. Operations & Financial Management Oversee pricing strategies, inventory management, and inventory turns. Manage accounts receivable, credit risks, and cost controls. Prepare and manage annual, monthly, and weekly operational plans and bud

title: Manager of Corporate Accounting
description: Venor is proud to partner with Graybar Canada on their search for a Manager of Corporate Accounting to join their head office team in Halifax, NS. Graybar Canada is a leader in the distribution of high-quality electrical, automation, and telecommunications products, supporting these operations through supply chain management and logistics services. Graybar Canada operates more than 35 branches across Canada. As one of Canada's largest employee-owned companies, Graybar Canada has the power and stability of a big corporation and the integrity and drive of a neighborhood business. What You’ll Be Doing The Manager of Corporate Accounting will support the Director of Accounting and VP, Finance in managing the day-to-day operations of the finance function for the organization. This is a key leadership role within the finance team, ensuring financial statements are accurate, compliant with accounting standards, and delivered on time. We're looking for a collaborative, hands-on professional who enjoys being involved in the details, isn't afraid to roll up their sleeves, and is eager to learn. Success in this role will come from a combination of technical capability, initiative, adaptability, and a genuine desire to contribute and gain new skills. Key Responsibilities Financial Reporting & Close: Support the complete accounting cycle: journal entries, accruals, prepayments, depreciation, account reconciliations & compiling working papers Support the Controller with monthly, quarterly,

title: Office Administrator & Bookkeeper
description: Venor is proud to partner with Archibald Drilling & Blasting in their search for an Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to join their team in Truro, Nova Scotia. Archibald Drilling & Blasting is a leading provider of drilling and blasting services, with more than 40 years of experience delivering safe, innovative, and reliable solutions across quarry operations, open-pit mining, civil construction, and marine projects. Known for its commitment to safety, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, Archibald supports some of the most complex and demanding projects throughout Atlantic Canada and beyond. As the company continues to grow, Archibald is seeking an organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to support day-to-day office operations and financial administration. This is a critical role within the organization, ideal for someone who enjoys variety in their work and thrives in a small business environment where they can make a meaningful impact. Reporting directly to leadership, the successful candidate will oversee office administration while managing core accounting and bookkeeping functions. This position offers the opportunity to help shape and improve internal processes as the company continues to evolve. What You'll Be Doing Manage the day-to-day administrative operations of the office Process payroll accurately and in a timely manner Manage accounts payable and accounts receivable functions Perform general bookkeeping duties an

title: Labour Relations Specialist
description: Venor is proud to partner with J.D. Irving, Limited in their search for a new Labour Relations Specialist to join their Human Resources team. J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) is a premier North American forestry and operations leader. What began in 1882 as a regional, family-owned enterprise has grown into a dynamic, coast-to-coast group of operating divisions with over 140 years of success. Recognized for building a highly efficient, vertically integrated supply chain, J.D. Irving, Limited delivers high-quality lumber, pulp and paper products, and sustainable forest management across Eastern Canada and the United States. At Grand Lake Timber Sawmill, Chipman, NB, JDI is proud of their strong legacy in sustainable forestry and high-quality wood products. Reporting to the HR Manager, the Labour Relations Specialist will play a key role in supporting positive union-management relations, ensuring compliance with collective agreements, and promoting a culture of fairness and accountability. They will support the business by interpreting and administering the collective agreement, maintaining a productive relationship with the Union, managing the grievance process, providing training and development to supervisors on company policies and labour relations best practices, assisting with progressive discipline practices, resolving complex labour relations issues and conducting workplace investigations in conjunction with Industrial Security Ltd. The ideal candidate has a strong background

title: EDI and API Integration Analyst
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for an EDI and API Integration Analyst to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team,  the EDI and API Integration Analyst is responsible for the operation, support, documentation, monitoring, onboarding, and continuous improvement of customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. This role ensures integration processes are reliable, well-documented, supportable, and aligned to business needs across transportation and logistics operations. As valued members of the expanding Data and Integration team, EDI and API Integration Analysts, along with current team of data integration professionals, collectively manage, support, document, monitor, onboard, and continuously enhance customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. Together, the data integrati

title: Power BI Report Developer
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for a Power BI Report Developer to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team, Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is seeking a Power BI Report Developer to design, develop, validate, and support trusted Power BI reports, dashboards, datasets, and analytics solutions across transportation, logistics, dispatch, billing, finance, safety, maintenance, customer service, and executive performance management. With a strong focus on report quality, performance, documentation, user acceptance testing, and adoption of standardized reporting practices, this role helps improve operational visibility, reduce manual reporting effort, and build a more mature analytics foundation for the business. The BI Report Developer is responsible for designing, developing, testing, validating, deploying, and supporting business intelligence repor

title: Marketing Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with Fidelis Law in their search for a Marketing Coordinator to join their team in Moncton, NB Fidelis Law is a dedicated personal injury firm with offices in Moncton and Edmundston, New Brunswick. The firm is committed to providing exceptional legal representation to personal injury victims while delivering personalized, compassionate service that ensures clients’ rights are protected and their voices are heard. As Fidelis Law continues to grow, they are seeking a Marketing Coordinator to support and execute marketing initiatives that strengthen brand awareness, enhance community presence, and support business development objectives. Reporting to the Partners of the firm, the Marketing Coordinator will play a key role in coordinating campaigns, creating engaging content, and supporting the day-to-day marketing activities that promote the firm's services and values. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and organized marketing professional who enjoys working in a collaborative environment and contributing to meaningful work that positively impacts clients and communities across New Brunswick. What you'll be doing: Support the planning, coordination, and execution of marketing campaigns across digital, print, social media, and community engagement channels. Assist in establishing marketing plans and objectives that align with the firm's business initiatives and growth strategies. Create and manage content for social media platforms, website u

title: Vice President of Finance
description: Venor is excited to partner with the Nexco Group on their search for a Vice President of Finance to join their team based in Dartmouth, NS. Nexco Group is a proudly Nova Scotian, integrated group of companies delivering trusted solutions across civil construction, traffic control, and infrastructure services. Through its divisions, Nexco Construction, Nexco Traffic Control, and Nexco Environmental, the company plays a vital role in building and maintaining the infrastructure that supports communities across Atlantic Canada. With expertise spanning mass excavation, site development, demolition, road building, highway structures and bridges, retaining walls, and traffic management, Nexco Group is known for its commitment to safety, quality, reliability, and collaboration. Driven by a passion for excellence and a people-first approach, Nexco partners with clients to successfully deliver projects that contribute to Atlantic Canada's continued growth and development. About the Role: We are seeking a highly strategic Vice President of Finance to provide executive-level oversight for the Nexco Group. As the ultimate owner of our financial vision, you will drive big-picture planning, manage complex cash flow and risk, and optimize internal controls to ensure sustainable, profitable growth across Nexco and a subsidiary of companies. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Financial Leadership & Planning Serve as the primary financial visionary for the Nexco Group, aligning financial strategie

title: Supply Chain Manager
description: SureShot Solutions designs, manufactures, and markets the most innovative dispensers of beverages and beverage add-ins, quenching the thirst of anyone looking for a better way to serve refreshments. With many industry firsts, they are passionate about identifying customer needs, and are tenacious about providing breakthrough solutions to address them. Over the years, they have proudly collaborated with top quick-serve restaurant chains and convenience retailers worldwide to design, engineer, manufacture, and market dispensers for beverages and beverage enhancers. At the heart of their success lies a deep-rooted commitment to excellence, where innovation meets unparalleled craftsmanship. Adaptability to change and a commitment to ensuring a safe workplace while delivering top-notch quality results are qualities SureShot holds in high regard. On behalf of SureShot, we are recruiting a Supply Chain Manager to lead key functional areas including procurement, inventory performance management, planning, and scheduling, while providing direct leadership to the Logistics Lead & Scheduler and the Purchasing Agent. Are you a strategic and systems-savvy supply chain professional who thrives on optimizing operations, leading teams, and delivering results? Reporting directly to the Vice President of Finance and Administration, this role is a critical member of the Supply Chain Management (SCM) Team and plays a central role in driving operational efficiency and supplier performance. Description

Hospitality is in your blood! 

A runner is the dynamic link that keeps the team moving forward seamlessly. Whether it's delivering crucial items or swiftly executing tasks, their speed and efficiency are essential for the team's success.

Please note that this is a part time position on evenings + weekends 

 

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Communicate effectively with staff to coordinate the timely preparation and delivery of requests.
Assist in maintaining cleanliness and organization, including clearing and resetting tables as needed.
Collaborate with servers to address any guest requests or concerns promptly and professionally.
Monitor and replenish supplies, such as condiments and utensils, as necessary to support efficient service.

Home » Employment Opportunities

Amal is an innovative and vibrant organization providing a diverse range of programs and services. We pride ourselves on our creative approaches to meeting the needs of young people, families and communities, and work hard to provide responsive, respectful and effective support and intervention.

We employ individuals in many disciplines, including social work, accounting, therapy, office administration and human resources, with our largest employee group consisting of child and youth care workers. Our workforce is regularly expanding as our organization grows.

If you are enthusiastic, passionate, committed to excellence and willing to work in a Growth Culture, then Amal may be the place for you.

Send your resume and cover letter to careers@amalnl.ca 

ABOUT

The vision of Amal Youth and Family Centre is to become a centre of hope, and a warm, welcoming, respectful and diverse organization in which children, youth and families from myriad backgrounds thrive and succeed together.

JUNIOR MECHANICAL ENGINEER - C-CORE

At C-CORE, we are committed to investing in people and creating a dynamic and rewarding employment experience. We are seeking a Junior Mechanical Engineer to join our Oceans & Energy Team. This position is based in St. John’s, NL.

If you have a passion for complex design solutions, hands-on fabrication, and contributing to cutting-edge R&D projects from concept to commissioning, this opportunity may be for you. Are you enthusiastic about engineering, attentive to detail, and motivated to support a diverse team of professionals in a dynamic environment? If so, we want you on our team!

About C-CORE

C-CORE conducts R&D and provides research-based advisory services, delivering innovative engineering and technology solutions to national and international clients. For over 50 years, we have built a world-class team of professionals in Remote Sensing and Oceans & Energy. We are global leaders in Earth Observation and geospatial intelligence, with our flagship platform Coresight driving major contracts in defense, environmental monitoring, and advanced R&D.

About Wedgwood Insurance

At Wedgwood Insurance, our core values of We Care, We Lead, We Improve, and We Deliver are reflected in everything we do. It’s how we’ve become the most trusted insurance broker in Newfoundland & Labrador and have been part of our foundation for over 45 years. We invest in our team by promoting personal and professional development, providing amazing benefits, and fostering a culture of respect and pride in our work. And it shows as we’ve been named one of the Best Places to Work in Atlantic Canada by Atlantic Business Magazine!

We are looking for a System Specialist to join our St. John’s office.

Our Career Opportunity – System Specialist

This isn’t a support desk role. As Systems Specialist, you are the person who keeps the engine running. You’ll own the operation, maintenance, and optimization of our brokerage management system – primarily Applied EPIC – along with the supporting platforms our team depends on every day.

Managing, ordering, and stocking of all office supplies;

 Data Analyst

 

Newfoundland and Labrador Medical Association (NLMA)

 

ethree Consulting is proud to partner with the Newfoundland and Labrador Medical Association in their search for a Data Analyst with The Family Practice Renewal Program (FPRP), a joint initiative of the NLMA and the Department of Health and Community Services (DHCS). The NLMA functions as the employer for staff of FPRP.

 

Who We Are

The Newfoundland and Labrador Medical Association (NLMA) is the united voice of physicians across the province. As a member-driven, non-profit organization, we advance the political, clinical, and economic interests of the medical profession while championing a fair, accessible, and equitable health care system for all residents.

 

We believe that strong advocacy, strategic action, and a deep commitment to equity not only improve the working lives of physicians, but they also directly enhance patient care and health outcomes across the province. Through advocacy, collaboration, and leadership, we help shape the future of health care in Newfoundland and Labrador, ensuring the system evolves to meet the needs of patients, providers, and communities.

 

Our mission drives us to innovate with those we serve and to build a health system that is accessible, sustainable, and trusted by all Newfoundlande

Administrative Assistant

NSB Omega is searching for an Administrative Assistant for our Client in Sarnia ON.


Assignment Description

• Provides administrative support to a department or individual. Duties may include: typing, filing, answering phones, scheduling, calendaring, record keeping, coordinating meetings and conferences, obtaining supplies and/or sorting/distributing mail.

• Must be able to multi-task and prioritize. 

• Strong communication skills and organizational skills are required. 

• Proficiency with MS Office required.

• Works with moderate work direction, and can identify issues/problems but may need assistance in resolving. 

• This position would typically include senior Administrative Assistant who is able to manage, train and mentor other office staff, lead projects, handle confidential and sensitive data. 
• Nature of the job is generally creating rather than maintaining and developing rather than monitoring.

Materialplanlegger
Materialplanlegger

Omega 365 Consulting er en ledende leverandør av dyktig prosjektpersonell til aktører innen olje, gass, fornybar energi og infrastruktur. Vi tilbyr et bredt utvalg av konsulentoppdrag, inkludert mange av de mest spennende prosjektene i Norge og i utlandet.




Detaljer:

Varighet: ASAP - 1-2 år
Lokasjon: Fokserød, Sandefjord




Beskrivelse av oppdraget

Etablere, bruke og vedlikeholde gruppens prosedyrer og retningslinjer innen materialplanlegging.

Optimalisere prosjektets forvaltning av kjøpte varer og tjenester.

Koordinere tverrfaglig arbeid i forbindelse med leveranser av materiell.

Motta MTO-er fra Engineering og opprette innkjøpsrekvisisjoner i ERP-system.

Overvåke innkjøpsaktiviteter med hensyn til ordre og leveranser, med fokus på installasjonstidspunkt.

Varsle om avvik og problemstillinger relatert til innkjøp og leveranser.

Informere om status og fremdrift i relevante møter og fora.

Informere om forsinkelser på innkjøpt materiell til fabrikasjon og installasjon.

Utarbeide status- og shortage-rapporter for innkjøpt materiell i prosjektet.

Knytte materiell opp mot arbeidsordrer (AO) i ERP-system.

Koordinere forsendelser av materiell til onshore-base i henhold til installasjonsplan.


Kvalifikasjoner

Erfaring fra arbeid innen materialplanlegging, logistikk eller tilsvarende rolle.

Application Rationalization - Portfolio Analyst

Omega 365 Consulting is a leading supplier of highly skilled project personnel in the oil and gas, renewable energy, and infrastructure sectors. We offer a wide range of consultant assignments, featuring many of the most exciting projects in Norway and internationally.




Details

Duration: 02.08.2026-28.01.2027
Location: Bergen, Stavanger




Assignment Description

Application Rationalization is the strategic process of evaluating client’s software portfolio to determine which applications should be kept, replaced, retired, or consolidated. The objective is to identify redundancies and underperforming tools to reduce IT cost, simplify the application landscape, and improve operational efficiency. A key outcome is to strengthen transparency between application cost, technical fitness, and business value, enabling measurable value creation and alignment with current business goals.


Role and scope of work:

The Digital Portfolio Analyst will support the business‑ and portfolio‑oriented aspects of the Application Rationalization initiative.

The role focuses on understanding how applications are used, what value they deliver, and how cost relates to business impact.

Conduct structured user and stakeholder interviews wit

title: Manager, Corporate Accounting & Reporting
description: Atlantic Corporation Limited (ACL), part of the Shannon Group of Companies, is seeking a Manager, Corporate Accounting & Reporting to join their team in Halifax. This is a unique opportunity for a CPA who is looking to move beyond a traditional accounting role and gain exposure to a broad portfolio of privately held businesses spanning transportation, healthcare, real estate, construction, information technology, and more. From industry-leading brands such as Seaboard Transport and Shannex to an extensive real estate portfolio, ACL serves as the corporate office supporting a diverse and growing group of entrepreneurial companies. The ACL Finance team sits at a unique intersection of operations, governance, executive leadership, shareholders, and the Board of Directors - providing both strategic support and critical oversight across the organization. For the right person, this role offers something few accounting opportunities can: a front-row seat to how successful businesses operate, grow, acquire, and evolve. Few organizations can offer the breadth of experience and exposure available through ACL. This team helps shape the future of one of Atlantic Canada's most successful privately held business groups. There truly is no other experience like it in Nova Scotia! This is a rare opportunity to see an entire business from the centre of it. The Opportunity: Reporting to the Senior Director, Finance & Accounting at ACL, with a dotted-line relationship to the Director of Finance

description: Who is COVE? COVE is a global technology partner advancing high-impact solutions from seabed to space. COVE transforms innovation into market-ready technologies through testing, commercialization support, market expertise and access to critical infrastructure. Leveraging its networks and mission-driven platform, COVE connects investors, innovators, industry, and the research community to drive cutting-edge capabilities for commercial and defence applications. Based in Canada and operating internationally, COVE supports the full lifecycle of maritime and multi-domain technologies – from research and development to manufacturing, integration, and export – linking Canadian innovation to global markets. COVE has built a globally recognized innovation platform, with an ecosystem of more than 700 organizations spanning startups, multinational firms, researchers, Indigenous partners, and public sector collaborators. Through its programs, facilities, and partnerships, COVE enables real-world testing and validation, strengthens supply chains, and accelerates technology adoption in complex operating environments. With strong connections across the North Atlantic, the United States, and the Arctic, and as part of international networks including NATO’s Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic (DIANA), COVE plays a key role in advancing technologies that support economic growth, environmental sustainability, and global security. title: Senior Full Stack Developer
description:
New
Senior Full Stack Developer
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role: 

Mechanical engineering teams rely on a complex ecosystem of CAD, PLM, and enterprise systems to design, review, and manufacture products. At CoLab, we're building software that fits naturally into those workflows.

As a Senior Full Stack Developer, you'll play a key role in making that possible. You'll build the integrations and platform capabilities that connect CoLab with the systems our customers use every day, helping engineering organizations review designs faster, collaborate more effectively, and make better decision

title: FastTrackHER Pilot Participant
description:
Skip to content
EMPLOYER SERVICES
CAREER & YOUTH SERVICES
ABOUT
NEWS & EVENTS
CONTACT
CAREER AND YOUTH SERVICES

Equiforce provides innovative, responsive, and industry-informed workforce development initiatives that build equitable pathways into the industries shaping Newfoundland and Labrador’s future and connect women and gender-diverse individuals with opportunity.

CAREER PROGRAMS
FASTTRACKHER

An innovative workforce readiness initiative aimed at preparing women for high-demand industries. FastTrackHER is a new pilot in development for 2026. Short, intensive, and industry-informed, it creates pathways into underrepresented fields through sector connections, relevant certifications, and direct insight into emerging industry opportunities. The first pilot focuses on Newfoundland and Labrador’s construction sector in the Avalon region, with future iterations expanding to other industries across the province.

PATHWAYS TO PLACE

A province-wide pilot launching in 2026 that connects newcomer and immigrant women with eight-week, paid, work-integrated learning placements in local organizations. Participants gain workplace readiness skills, valuable Canadian experience, professional networks, and wraparound supports that promote success and belonging. Employers, in turn, strengthen their capacity through structured guidance that supports inclusive hiring, onboarding, and workplace practices.

For more information and to learn how to participate, click here.  

CAREER PATHWAYS

SUMMARY OF DUTIES:

Under the general supervision of the Senior Consular Program Officer, the Consular Assistant provides administrative support services for the passport and citizenship programs.

The Consular Assistant:

Receives and processes passport applications in person, including document review, data entry, fee collection, and client follow-up.
Provides urgent passport services by handling urgent files and printing emergency travel documents, temporary passports, and observation labels.
Provides citizenship services by reviewing citizenship applications and supporting documentation.
Supports internal operations of the consular section by designing and implementing internal tracking documents.
Responds to general client inquiries in person, by telephone

title: Senior Consultant, People & Change
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference. At KPMG, you’ll join a team of diverse and dedicated problem solvers, connected by a common cause: turning insight into opportunity for clients and communities around the world.  Are you a talented leader with a proven track record for motivating teams and delivering exceptional client service? Our People & Change team in Halifax is looking for a professional like you with the skills and drive to make a real difference. KPMG’s People & Change Advisory Practice transforms organizations by changing the way people lead, manage and develop. In an age of constant disruption, game-changing trends, and perpetual transformation we help our clients drive business value and achieve strategic goals through the optimization of their people and organizational agenda. We help CHROs make their function future ready. Our practitioners deliver strategic and operational consulting services related to organizational effectiveness and change, human resource planning and transformation. From developing talent strategies to optimizing and reshaping both the workforce and HR function, our knowledgeable advice enables improved performance from our clients’ people and their organizations

This is a full-time position with no fixed end date, subject to external funding.

ACENET is seeking a Research Consultant in Quantum Computing to assist academic and industry clients in understanding, evaluating, and applying emerging quantum computing technologies alongside advanced computing resources (HPC, AI, Cloud) available through ACENET and the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

This role is focused on enabling early exploration and responsible adoption of quantum computing, rather than hardware development or theoretical research. The successful candidate will have access to quantum computing platforms to support hands-on experimentation and applied projects.

ACENET’s central mission is to help researchers and innovators get their work done more efficiently by providing access to advanced computing resources and making those resources easier to use.

Maintain confidential, trauma-informed, and culturally responsive approaches to working with diverse populations.

Reporting to the Manager of Employment Services, the Career Practitioner is responsible for assisting newcomers to Canada with preparing for, obtaining and maintaining meaningful employment in Newfoundland and Labrador. The position aims to expand employment assistance, work readiness support, and entrepreneurship services for newcomers and employers in rural Newfoundland and Labrador, specifically in Corner Brook and the Western region. Our Approach

Stewart McKelvey has a long history of being a leader in Atlantic Canada. And being a leader means embracing innovation.

We changed the traditional approach to law student recruitment in Atlantic Canada. Typically, Articled Clerks wait for “hire-back” decisions well into their articling year.

Stewart McKelvey is thinking differently.

We believe that success in the practice of law requires making a commitment for more than one summer or one year. Our innovative approach removes the traditional “hire-back” decision-making process, typically done at the end of the articling year. A 2L Summer Student will advance to becoming an Articled Clerk, then directly to an Associate at Stewart McKelvey.

Will support validation and data quality efforts for Safety Instrumented Systems (SIS) and control systems within a large, regulated industrial environment. The primary focus of this role is Digital Cause & Effect (DiCE) automated metrics table population, Cause & Effect (C&E) document tag comparison, and control system tag validation. Must have experience with control systems, SIS (safety instrumented systems), cause & effect testing, documentation and validation.

Responsibilities:
• Populate and maintain automated DiCE metrics tables using established templates and validation rules
• Clean, organize, and transform large datasets to ensure accurate metrics reporting
• Perform consistency checks to verify alignment across data sources
• Track progress against defined validation milestones and report status to project leads
• C&E Documentation Validation
• Execute tag-by-tag comparisons between Cause & Effect (C&E) documentation and reference sources (e.g., instrument lists, I/O lists, logic narratives)
• Identify discrepancies including missing tags, naming mismatches, duplicate entries, or incorrect associations
• Log and document findings in structured tracking tools with clear, repeatable evidence
• SIS / Controls Tag Validation
• Validate SIS and control system tags for completeness

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help engineering teams bring life-changing products to the world years sooner. Our platform is the world’s first Design Engagement System (DES) - a category defining product that Engineering teams use to engage in meaningful, productive design conversations, catch preventable mistakes, and get to market faster. Our customers include the largest engineering organizations in the world spanning from industrial equipment, consumer products, automotive, aerospace & defense, and shipbuilding industries.

About the role

As a Senior Customer Success Manager, you will lead the post-sale customer journey for a portfolio of our largest Enterprise customer, with a focus on driving measurable business results, product adoption, long-term retention. You’ll partner closely with engineering and business stakeholders to align execution to customer goals, drive product adoption, and ensure CoLab becomes integral to customers’ workflows.

You’ll be part of a cross-functional account team, working closely with Customer Engineering Advisors to deliver technical enablement and collaborating with Sales partners to support retention and expansion. This is a growth-oriented role for someone who thrives in fast-paced, technical environments and has a proven track record of driving product adoption to influence long-term retention and satisfaction.

Dreaming, designing, and delivering isn’t just a mantra for us — it’s a way of life. We believe that technology is the conduit, but our people – they are the connection that truly makes the magic happen!

Our Culture
We are a human-centered culture where we prioritize your well-being and are invested in helping you reach your fullest potential. We’re not only focused on achieving our goals — we’re dedicated to helping you achieve yours.

If you’re relentlessly curious, driven to make a difference, and collaborative at the core, then you belong with us.

The Angular Developer will play a key role in the Province’s ConnectPEI Enterprise Client Relationship Management (eCRM) Program, a multi-year initiative focused on modernizing and consolidating legacy CRM applications into a scalable, reusable platform. Working within an Agile delivery team, the successful candidate will develop and maintain enterprise-grade

As the Senior Project Manager, Regulatory Remediation you are responsible for leading, directing, and overseeing a data protection regulatory program to ensure enterprise-wide compliance objectives are accomplished within approved timeframes, scope, and budget. title: Finance & Business Process Analyst
description: Venor is proud to partner with ReelData AI on their search for a Finance & Business Process Analyst to join their growing team in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia. ReelData AI is transforming land-based aquaculture through the application of artificial intelligence, computer vision, and data-driven technology. Their innovative platform helps fish farms optimize feeding, improve fish health, and increase operational efficiency, supporting a more sustainable future for aquaculture around the world. As the company continues to grow, they are seeking a Finance & Business Process Analyst to help build greater operational leverage across finance, reporting, people operations, and internal business processes. This is a highly collaborative role that works closely with the CFO while partnering with leaders across production, deployment, engineering, product, and commercial teams to improve the systems that keep the business running efficiently. This is a hybrid position based out of ReelData AI's Dartmouth office. Candidates must be based in Canada and able to work regularly from the Halifax Regional Municipality. The Opportunity This role is ideal for someone who enjoys bringing structure to fast-moving environments. You'll combine analytical thinking with practical execution, helping streamline recurring business processes, improve reporting, coordinate operational activities, and identify opportunities to leverage automation and AI. Success in this role requires someone who is highly organi

title: Financial Analyst
description: Venor is proud to partner with Champlain Seafood (www.champlainseafood.com) who are seeking a Financial Analyst to join their team in Moncton, New Brunswick. Champlain Seafood is the largest processor of lobster and snow crab sourced from the Canadian Maritimes and American Northeast. The company has been recognized as one of the Best Managed Companies in Canada and one of the Best Places to work in Atlantic Canada over the last three years. With multiple processing operations and continued growth across the business, the organization is investing further in the financial tools and insights that support day-to-day operations and long-term planning. Working alongside an experienced corporate finance group and a strong plant leadership team, this role will help strengthen data visibility across production, costing, and margins. It’s an opportunity for a Financial Analyst to become embedded within the operation, working closely with plant teams to better capture and understand performance drivers and support informed decision making. The successful candidate will help connect plant operations with corporate finance, using strong analytical skills and practical insight to support a growing production platform focused on efficiency, continuous improvement, and long-term success. What You’ll Be Doing Act as the financial bridge between corporate finance and plant operations, supporting Controllers, senior finance leaders, and the VP of Production. Take ownership of detailed cost ac

Piping Designers

 

We're seeking skilled and detail-oriented Piping Designers, of all levels, to join our Design & Consulting team in supporting innovative shipbuilding and marine engineering projects!

 

Our team is split into two main divisions that complement each other, 3D modeling and Manufacturing information (MI). The 3D model team takes engineering documents and turns them into a fully integrated digital ship. The MI team takes the 3D model and produces 2D drawings that the shipyard production facilities can fabricate from.

 

In this role, you’ll play a key part in developing piping systems and layouts for new vessel construction and refit programs. Working closely with other disciplines, production teams, and regulatory bodies, you’ll help bring complex designs to life using advanced 3D modeling tools and industry software to produce 2D drawings.

Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. title: General Manager
description:
Ashburn Golf Club is one of Atlantic Canada’s premier private clubs, combining over a century of tradition with a modern and engaging member experience across Halifax and Fall River. Established in 1922 and designed by renowned Canadian architect Stanley Thompson, Ashburn is known for its championship-calibre golf and strong, community-oriented culture.

With two courses in Halifax and Fall River, along with dining, events, and year-round programming, the Club offers a dynamic and well-rounded experience supported by an engaged membership and a strong governance model.

As the Club enters an exciting period of growth, Ashburn Golf Club is seeking a strategic and engaging General Manager (GM) to lead the organization through this next phase and ensure continued operational excellence and long-term success.

What does this role entail?

Reporting to the Board of Directors, you will be responsible for setting and executing the Club’s strategic direction, with full accountability for financial performance, operations, and member experience. You will lead all core functions, including golf operations, food and beverage, facilities, membership services, and administration, while fostering strong stakeholder relationships and operational excellence.

title: President
description:
Sault College is actively seeking an innovative and people-centred President to lead the College into its next chapter, guided by a new strategic plan. The President will guide a dedicated team in fostering an inclusive, student centred culture while navigating a rapidly evolving post secondary landscape. This is a pivotal opportunity to strengthen academic excellence, deepen community partnerships, and position Sault College as a resilient and trusted anchor institution for Sault Ste. Marie and Northern Ontario.

Sault College
Sault College has a proud history characterized by tremendous growth and spanning over more than five decades, evolving from a Vocational Centre opening in 1965 to the College it is today. Each year, the College responds to the postsecondary educational needs of more than 4,000 part-time and full-time students and continues to add new programs across all disciplines. Sault College currently offers 57 programs including 16 College Certificates; 29 Ontario College Diplomas; 4 Ontario College Advanced Diplomas; 6 Ontario Graduate Certificates; and 2 Bachelor’s Degrees. The Sault College campus is home to a diverse array of new and upgraded educational facilities including the Waterfront Adventure Centre, Motive Power Centre, and iE3.

In addition to serving the student body, Sault College has a strong presence in the community and across Northern Ontario. As one of the community’s largest employers, Sault College’s direct economic impact to the Algoma Reg

title: Director, Fire Services
location: West Hants Regional Municipality, Nova Scotia, CA
description:
West Hants Regional Municipality is seeking an accomplished fire services professional and people-focused leader to serve as its next Director of Fire Services.

West Hants Regional Municipality

West Hants Regional Municipality (WHRM) is a dynamic and growing community in Hants County, Nova Scotia, formed through the amalgamation of the former Town of Windsor and the Municipality of the District of West Hants in 2020. Strategically situated along the Avon and St. Croix Rivers, the region offers a compelling blend of small-town charm and regional connectivity, with easy access to Halifax and the broader Atlantic Canadian economy. Home to a population of just over 19,500, WHRM combines the close-knit feel of its key communities, including Windsor and Hantsport, with the space and character of its surrounding rural landscapes.
West Hants Regional Municipality is a mix of 52 rural and urban communities. It is the first region to welcome you as you arrive in the Annapolis Valley from Halifax.  With more than 1,250 square kms, there is something in every nook and cranny to inspire you to explore more and linger longer. West Hants borders on Kings County to the northwest and Halifax County to the south.

The Role 

Under the direction of the Chief Administrative Officer, the Director of Fire Services is responsible for the oversight of all fire services that support the Municipality and would collaborate with and support protective services such as Ground Search and Rescue (GSAR),

Founding Sales Lead
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

Help build the commercial engine of an AI-powered safety company with real product, real customers, and industrial pilots deploying this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. We've shipped product to 16 countries, we're well-funded, and we're deploying to industrial pilot sites this summer across oil & gas, marine, manufacturing, and heavy industry.

We're looking for a Founding Sales Lead to help us build our sales motion from the ground up. This isn't cold-calling from a script, it's figuring out how to sell a category-defining product into industries where the buyers are safety managers, operations leads, and EHS directors.


‍

What You’ll Do

This is not a traditional BDR role, it’s a chance to help build the commercial engine of a deep-tech startup.

You will:

Lead sales outreach, qualification, and discovery with enterprise and mid-market industrial customers.
Run customer discovery to deeply understand problems, workflows, and buying processes.
Help shape our storytelling, messaging, and positioning based on real customer insights.
Work with the founding team to build and refine landing pages, funnels, and experiments to test what resonates.
Drive top-of-funnel pipeline, schedule demos, and support enterprise sales cycles.
Collaborate with marketing to grow ou

title: Founding Firmware Developer
description:
Home
Careers
Contact
View All Openings
Founding Firmware Developer
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

First firmware hire at an AI-powered safety company with a major oil & gas contract and industrial pilots launching this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. Our headsets suppress harmful noise while preserving speech, alarms, and environmental cues, paired with a software platform that gives safety teams live exposure data and actionable insights.

We've already shipped 400 consumer headsets to 16 countries, completed 4 hardware revisions in 12 months, and navigated international manufacturing from Newfoundland. We've secured a major R&D contract and have 5 industrial pilots committed for this summer.

As our first dedicated firmware developer, you won't just write code, you'll own the firmware architecture, make critical decisions about reliability, connectivity, and power management, and ship software that performs in industrial conditions where failure isn't an option. You'll work directly with our CEO and CTO.



‍AI-Native Development

We're an AI-native team. You'll use Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot daily to accelerate development, generating drivers, test harnesses, utilities, and boilerplate, then critically review and harden that code for production embedded systems. AI isn't a replacement for deep embedded engineering judgment. It's a multiplier.

Our headsets suppress harmful noise while preserving speech, alarms, and environmental cues, paired with a software platform that gives safety teams live exposure data and actionable insights.

We've already shipped 400 consumer headsets to 16 countries, completed 4 hardware revisions in 12 months, and navigated international manufacturing from Newfoundland. We've secured a major R&D contract and have 5 industrial pilots committed for this summer.

As our first dedicated hardware developer, you'll own the hardware stack. You'll design boards, bring them up in the lab, validate them in the field, and ensure they perform reliably on offshore rigs, factory floors, and vessel decks.

Design and iterate on schematics and PCB layouts for compact, industrial-grade IoT headset.
Conduct lab bring-up, debug boards, and validate hardware functionality.
Build test setups and collect data to inform hardware and firmware design decisions.
Collaborate with firmware, software, and product teams to ensure seamless integration.

Wills & Estates Associate Lawyer | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

ABOUT COX & PALMER

200 lawyers strong, with over a century of experience serving clients. Cox & Palmer is a full-service regional firm providing advice to individuals and businesses in a broad range of sectors across all major industries.

We are committed to providing the highest quality of services to our clients. We are also committed to fostering an inclusive culture that values diverse perspectives and equitable opportunities.

 

THE OPPORTUNITY

Our Halifax office is excited to welcome an Associate Lawyer to our growing Wills & Estates practice group.  In this role, you will work closely with clients on a diverse range of matters, from drafting will and trusts to handling probate and estate litigation.  This practice group offers the opportunity to gain broader exposure to more sophisticated or high value estates including wealth transfers and complex multi-generational estate plan

Enaimco’s mission is to give heavy industry the ability to manage and understand their assets remotely — starting with subsea, one of the world’s toughest environments. Contribute to the evolution of Enaimco’s design system to improve consistency and scalability across the platform.

Jobs, Growth and Rural Development

Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking an experienced Property Paralegal to join our Charlottetown office. The ideal candidate will bring a strong background in residential real estate transactions, excellent organizational skills, and the ability to manage files in a fast-paced environment. While we value collaboration and ongoing development, this role is best suited for someone who can confidently support property transactions with minimal supervision.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES


Working as part of a collaborative team, you will:

Assist lawyers with all aspects of residential property files;
Draft documentation for property transactions;
Act and a liaison between the firm and clients, providing clear and timely communication on the status of transactions;
Manage ordering title searches and liaising with external partners (banks, other law firms, Realtors, IRAC and others);

Humber Polytechnic is seeking a strategic and entrepreneurial leader who views financial leadership as a platform for value creation, institutional transformation, and long-term public impact to serve as the next Vice President, Administration & Chief Financial Officer.

Humber Polytechnic
Located in Toronto, one of the most diverse cities in the world, Humber Polytechnic is a leading Canadian post-secondary institution recognized for its innovative, polytechnic approach to education. As an Institute of Technology and Advanced Learning and a founding member of Polytechnics Canada, Humber serves more than 76,000 full-time and part-time learners across six Faculties and four campuses. The institution offers an extensive range of credentials, from apprenticeships and diplomas to bachelor’s and master’s degrees, all grounded in experiential, career-focused learning that prepares graduates to thrive in a rapidly evolving workforce.

For over 50 years, Humber has delivered a rich academic and student experience rooted in teaching excellence, applied research, and strong industry connections. Its award-winning faculty bring deep academic expertise and real-world experience into state-of-the-art labs and classrooms that mirror professional environments. Through robust partnerships with industry, community organizations, and global institutions, Humber ensures students gain meaningful work-integrated learning opportunities and access to influential networks.

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role owns paid distribution at CoLab. You’ll own the performance, growth, and evolution of our paid media program — with LinkedIn as the primary channel, but not the only one. Your job is to take what’s already working, improve it month over month, and expand into new channels that help us reach the right buyers in new ways.

Chief Administrative Officer

Higher Talent is excited to conduct an expedited search for a motivated and community‑focused

 Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) for the Town of Witless Bay, Newfoundland and Labrador.

WHO WE ARE: 

The Town of Witless Bay is not just a great place to visit but is also a great community to live and do business.  Located on Route 10 as a part of The Irish Loop, Witless Bay is a small, scenic, traditional Newfoundland outport community with a population of 1640 that offers an abundance of Natural Tourism. Hiking, camping, and fishing can be found along the East Coast Trail. Sea kayaking is very popular in the Witless Bay area. For the amateur looking for adventure, there are several businesses offering boat and sea kayaking tours in the area. Icebergs, whales, and incredible bird colonies are just some of the attractions this scenic area has to offer. The Witless Bay Ecological Reserve is a must see! To learn more about us, please click on the Town of Witless Bay.

THE ROLE: 

Reporting to the Town Council, we are seeking a motivated and community‑focused Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) which is a senior management position responsible for the strategic leadership and efficient delivery of all the Town’s administrative and operational services. Job description
 HRBP – Middle East

Who are we?
At Global Maritime, our remit is to meet the challenges of the sea. What truly sets Global Maritime apart in the world of maritime engineering consultancy is the combination of the intellectually stimulating work itself, our deeply collaborative culture, and the uniquely supportive leadership. Global Maritime stands out as the premier destination for maritime engineers due to our unique blend and unparalleled technical opportunities – the opportunity to shape the industry's future on globally significant, cutting-edge projects – within a supportive and collaborative international community. Combined with expert leadership that actively mentors your growth within a trusting environment.
 
Welcoming you to our team
You will join a high-performing regional team covering Azerbaijan, the U.A.E., Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Egypt. Help Shape the Future of Hospice and Palliative Care on Nova Scotia’s South Shore

The South Shore Hospice Palliative Care Society (SSHPCS) is a community‑founded, donor‑supported charitable organization advancing compassionate, accessible end‑of‑life care for residents of Lunenburg and Queens Counties. Through the generosity of donors and in partnership with the Nova Scotia Health Palliative Care Services team, they support and promote compassionate care for individuals and their loved ones who are living with a life-limiting illness.

Through sustained grassroots leadership and community support, SSHPCS has played a pivotal role in improving access to palliative and hospice care, most notably securing five designated hospice beds at Fishermen’s Memorial Hospital. 

SSHPCS is now entering a pivotal new chapter and is focused on expanding palliative and hospice bed capacity across the South Shore and advancing toward a future freestanding hospice facility, a transformational opportunity that will reshape end‑of‑life care in the regio

Overview



At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

 

Our Governance, Risk and Compliance Services (GRCS) professionals provide a range of advisory services to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the risk function, regulatory programs, regulatory compliance functions, operational risk, enterprise risk management programs and third-party relationships governance initiatives. We analyze and assess an organization’s business strategies and related business processes and systems to help management understand and address business objectives, regulatory requirements, risks, controls, and improvement opportunities. Our professionals bring both deep technical and industry experience, allowing clients to strengthen their key governance, risk management and compliance efforts while optimizing business performance.

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

 

Our EPM Practice, as one of the key pillars of Finance and Digital Transformation is looking for Anaplan, OneStream and Oracle experienced professionals to join our talented team in Canada. Our team’s experience have been helping Finance organizations transcend finance’s role as a record-keeper and become performance and enterprise-focused, or true business partners. We are trusted advisors and leaders in budgeting, forecasting, consolidation, reporting and analytics with a particular focus on implementing technology solutions to enable these processes.

Participate and lead scoping, implementation, and deployment projects.
Conduct client workshops, understand, and analyze business requirements, propose recommendations, and prepare solution blueprint.

Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

 

Are you a talented individual with a proven track record on executing project deliverables.  

 

Our Operations Mergers & Acquisition team in Vancouver is looking for a professional like you with the skills and drive to make a real difference.

 

At KPMG, our Deal Advisory Operations M&A practice works with corporate and private equity clients through some of their most critical business events. Within our Value Creation team, we help clients identify and realize operational, cash flow, and performance improvement opportunities associated with transactions, integrations, separations, and broader strategic initiatives.

Our team combines financial, operational, and commercial perspectives to help organizations improve performance, enhance operational effectiveness, and support value creation objectives across a range of industries and transaction environments.

This team has a global mandate and spans multiple industries, with a particular emphasis on Financial Institutions, where we focus on the review, design, enhancement, and integration of: Enterprise Risk Management; Operational Risk Management; Regulatory Compliance; Third-party Risk Management; Climate Risk Management; Enterprise / Operational Resilience; 1b Functions; and Control Testing Functions.
