This is an opportunity for a confident, self-directed sales professional who excels at building immediate trust, identifying customer needs, and closing business through a consultative, results-driven approach. Bath Fitter is a recognized leader in home improvement, known for its customer-first culture, operational excellence, and commitment to performance-driven growth. For more than 40 years, Bath Fitter has helped homeowners transform their bathrooms through innovative products, professional installation, and an exceptional customer experience. The company invests heavily in training, leadership development, and proven sales systems that enable team members to build rewarding and successful careers. The Sales Consultant plays a critical role in helping homeowners bring their renovation goals to life. Acting as a trusted advisor throughout the sales process, this individual will build relationships, understand customer needs, present tailored solutions, and guide clients through the decision-making process while delivering an outstanding customer experience. Great Canadian Docks is a leader in the marine industry, providing premium-quality waterfront solutions through its direct-to-market locations and a strong network of independent dealers spanning from Saskatchewan to Newfoundland. As the exclusive Central and Eastern Canadian distributor for EZ Dock, the industry leader in modular floating dock systems for more than 30 years, Great Canadian Docks delivers best-in-class marine solutions serving a broad range of clients from homeowners and marinas to parks, resorts, and government agencies. Great Canadian Docks has earned a strong industry reputation through its comprehensive product offerings, dedication to reliable solutions and exceptional service, and a focus on helping clients protect and maximize their waterfront investments. About the Role: Reporting to the Director of Sales, the Dock Sales and Service Specialist will play a crucial role in developing strategic partnerships, work with customers to understand their waterfront needs, and help design practical, real-world dock solutions. title: General Manager

With an established reputation for safety, reliability, and customer service, the organization is seeking a dynamic General Manager to lead operations, drive profitability, and support continued growth. This is an opportunity for an entrepreneurial leader who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys building high-performing teams, and has the business acumen to balance strategic growth with operational excellence. Reporting directly to ownership, the General Manager will have broad responsibility for the overall success of the business, including operations, financial performance, business development, and people leadership. The successful candidate will bring energy, accountability, and a hands-on approach to leadership, helping position the organization for its next stage of growth. title: Industry Strategy Lead
description:
Industry Strategy Lead
NL, Remote

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

Our Ideal Candidate

This role is a high-leverage operator reporting into the Director of Industry Strategy & Transformation, with direct exposure to revenue mentorship and account owners. This role exists to inject strategic execution into CoLab’s enterprise deals, sharpen focus to scale wins, translate platform capability into enterprise strategy, and ensure we maintain product market fit.

This is a deal-facing, customer-facing, and product-influencing role. In this role, you will drive consensus inside complex manufacturing organizat

 Job Posts Network Engineer

NETWORK ENGINEER

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Network Engineer to support store network lifecycle activities. This role focuses on managing and maintaining Cisco Meraki-based network environments, including device lifecycle tracking, configuration validation, and operational reporting. The ideal candidate brings foundational expertise in cloud-managed networking, demonstrates strong collaboration and communication skills, and can effectively prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment.

In this role, you will

Track and report on inventory KPIs related to network devices and lifecycle status
Monitor inventory levels and lifecycle stages of network hardware across store environments
Create, maintain, and update documentation related to network configurations, updates, and changes
Act as an escalation point for troubleshooting anomalies and identifying network-related issues
Validate and verify configurations of newly deployed Cisco Meraki devices
Ensure proper decommissioning and removal of lifecycle-completed devices from the Meraki Dashboard
Provide detailed and structured progress reports on network lifecycle activities to stakeholders
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including operations, engineering, and business stakeholders

What you’ll need to succeed (required skills)

7+ years of networking experience with a strong focus on Cisco

 Job Posts Cybersecurity Architect – Critical National Infrastructure

CYBERSECURITY ARCHITECT – CRITICAL NATIONAL INFRASTRUCTURE

About the role
As a Cybersecurity Architect – Critical National Infrastructure (CNI), you will make an impact by designing, improving, and operating security architecture and defense capabilities across complex cloud and hybrid environments. You will be a valued member of the cybersecurity team, working closely with cloud, infrastructure, and application teams to strengthen security posture and reduce organizational risk.

In this role, you will:

Design and implement security architectures across AWS and Azure environments, ensuring alignment with enterprise and regulatory standards
Lead and support advanced security operations, including monitoring, investigation, threat detection, and incident response
Develop and enhance detection capabilities across SIEM, EDR, and cloud-native tooling to improve accuracy and reduce noise
Drive incident response activities including triage, containment, remediation, and post-incident analysis
Partner with cross-functional teams to remediate vulnerabilities, harden systems, and reduce the overall attack surface

Work model:
We believe hybrid work is the way forward as we strive to provide flexibility wherever possible. Based on this role’s business requirements, this is a hybrid position requiring 2–3 days per week in a Cognizan

SR. AJO/AEP DEVELOPER

About the role
As a Senior Adobe Journey Optimizer Developer, you will make an impact by designing and delivering advanced, real-time customer engagement solutions using Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) technologies. You will be a valued member of the delivery team, working closely with architects, data teams, and business stakeholders to build scalable, high-performing customer journey solutions.

In this role, you will:

Lead the design and development of real-time, cross-channel customer journeys using Adobe Journey Optimizer
Translate complex business and marketing requirements into scalable and secure AEP solutions
Architect and implement AEP components including XDM schemas, datasets, profiles, and identity frameworks
Design and manage event-triggered and segment-based journeys, including logic for re-entry, throttling, and versioning
Enable seamless orchestration across channels such as email, push, SMS, in-app, and web

Work model
We strive to provide flexibility wherever possible. Based on this role’s business requirements, this is a remote position open to qualified applicants in Canada. Regardless of your working arrangement, we are here to support a healthy work-life balance through our wellbeing programs.

 Job Posts Co-Op Content Creator

CO-OP CONTENT CREATOR

Meet QuickFacts?
QuickFacts is a Software as a Service (SaaS) company that organizes information and presents it on a searchable web-based application. We develop intricate, innovative software that is simple to use and saves our clients hours each day. Currently, we provide the solution to the insurance industry, offering brokerages a one-stop location for organizational workflows as well as all their carrier underwriting information, including custom-made comparisons, documents, training, and helpful tools.

We seek a dynamic, results-driven Co-Op Content Creator to join our team. The Co-op Content Creator will gain hands-on experience extracting key information from insurance manuals, wordings, and supporting documents to enhance our platform. You’ll also develop skills in preparing forms in Adobe and organizing accessible pages for clients to find and access essential documents easily. As you collaborate with the Comparison Team, you’ll gain a foundational understanding of insurance and learn what our clients need to make their jobs easier. This role is perfect for detail-oriented individuals looking to build expertise in content management, document organization, and insurance industry essentials!

 Job Posts Product Owner, Global CRM

PRODUCT OWNER, GLOBAL CRM

Introduction
Welcome to Gallagher – a global community of people who bring bold ideas, deep expertise, and a shared commitment to doing what’s right. We help clients navigate complexity with confidence by empowering businesses, communities, and individuals to thrive. At Gallagher, you’ll find more than a job; you’ll find a culture built on trust, driven by collaboration, and sustained by the belief that we’re better together. Whether you join us in a client-facing role or as part of our brokerage division, our benefits and HR consulting division, or our corporate team, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your career, make an impact, and be part of something bigger. Experience a workplace where you’re encouraged to be yourself, supported to succeed, and inspired to keep learning. That’s what it means to live The Gallagher Way.

Overview
As our organization enters a critical growth phase — including M&A activity, technology consolidation, and an enterprise CRM transformation — the Product Owner will play a key role in delivering the Salesforce roadmap across our Specialty and Wholesale divisions.

The Product Owner is responsible for translating business needs into clear, actionable requirements and ensuring the Salesforce platform supports day-to-day sales and client management across the customer lifecycle.

BUSINESS PROCESS TRANSFORMATION LEAD (AI & AUTOMATION)

Job description

We have partnered with a Canadian-based marketing agency that is part of an independent Network of Agencies strategically located across the world to provide global reach and capabilities.

As a senior individual contributor within the department, you will be responsible for leading complex, high-impact process transformation initiatives. From identifying opportunities through to solution design, rapid validation, and implementation planning, you will work closely with stakeholders to shape and deliver transformation outcomes that combine process excellence with modern technology, including AI and automation.

You will act as a trusted advisor to stakeholders, using strong communication and influencing skills to diagnose business problems, challenge existing approaches, and design forward-looking, technology-enabled solutions. A key aspect of the role is defining how AI and automation can be applied to improve processes and supporting the organization in adopting these solutions effectively.

The Director, PAM Systems Support, drives operational excellence across the organization’s privileged access management ecosystem. RBC Insurance Technology is seeking to hire a Senior Process Automation Specialist for its Insurance Technology Platform Support team. The Insurance Technology Platform Support Team is a specialized unit dedicated to ensuring the optimal performance, availability, and resilience of IT applications used in the insurance line of business. With a unique blend of technical expertise and industry-specific knowledge, this team plays a critical role in ensuring the seamless operations of digital services that cater to both the business’s internal and external stakeholders.

The Senior Process Automation Specialist is Team Lead for the Process Analysts team within the Insurance Technology Platform Support – Operations Management Team, driving automation initiatives, optimizing workflows, and enhancing operational efficiency. This role will focus on identifying opportunities for automation, implementing process improvements, and ensuring seamless integration of automation solutions across operations, SRE, and operations management teams.  Job Posts ML Engineer, Agentic AI Risk & Compliance, GFT

ML ENGINEER, AGENTIC AI RISK & COMPLIANCE, GFT

Job Description
What is the opportunity?

RBC’s AI Innovation team, within Technology & Operations, designs and delivers agentic AI solutions that transform how Risk and Compliance functions operate. We engage stakeholders to identify pain points and build AI-powered solutions – from intelligent document analysis and automated report generation to virtual agents that handle complex, manually intensive tasks. Working alongside subject matter experts, product owners, and engineering teams, we leverage large language models, agentic frameworks, and cloud infrastructure to bring these solutions to production.

This is a roving, high-impact role that will require parachuting into any risk or compliance domain area to deliver AI solutions. It is ideal for someone who is energized by building AI-powered automation and decision-making solutions. The challenges will be a mix of building from scratch and integrating existing systems.

What will you do?

Development: Design, build, and deploy agentic AI systems using Python, large language models, and modern AI frameworks. You will prototype rapidly and iterate solutions into production-grade systems that can interpret natural language queries, retrieve and validate information, automate complex analysis, and triage alerts.

DIRECTOR INFORMATION AND DATA SECURITY (GLOBAL SECURITY)

Job Description
What is the Opportunity?

The Director of Data Security Technology Operations leads the operational excellence and technical support of RBC’s data security infrastructure. This role is responsible for the day-to-day operation, maintenance, and support of a diverse portfolio of on-premises and SaaS-based security technologies that enable RBC’s data protection and data loss prevention capabilities. You will lead a globally distributed team of technical support staff responsible for ensuring these systems remain available, optimized, and aligned with business and compliance requirements. Additionally, you will serve as a key operational liaison between business units and the security technology team, enabling smooth integration of data security solutions across the enterprise.

What Will You Do?

Lead, mentor, and develop a geographically dispersed team of data security technology specialists responsible for operating and maintaining endpoint and network-based security tooling
Oversee the troubleshooting, maintenance, and optimization of on-premises and cloud-based data security technologies
Develop and maintain standard operating procedures, runbooks, and technical documentation that enable consistent, reliable service delivery across all systems

 Job Posts Reporting Data Analyst

REPORTING DATA ANALYST

Embark on a rewarding career with Sobeys Inc., celebrated among Canada’s Top 100 employers where your unique contributions drive success.

Role Overview
The Retail Media Post-Campaign Reporting Analyst is responsible for generating insightful, data-driven reports for retail media digital campaigns across multiple channels and platforms. This role combines technical analytics expertise with a strong understanding of retail media performance and audience analytics, helping commercial and sales teams evaluate campaign effectiveness and optimize future media investments.

Sobeys is full of exciting opportunities, and we are always looking for bright new talent to join our team! We currently have a full-time opportunity for a Reporting Data Analyst. This role is based out of one our offices located in Halifax, NS.

#LI-Hybrid #LI-VJ1

Key Responsibilities

Develop and automate post-campaign reports for retail media campaigns across onsite, offsite, and in-store channels.
Pull, clean, and analyze large datasets from multiple sources including Snowflake, Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and reporting APIs (e.g., Meta, DV360, GA4).
Build, maintain, and enhance data workflows and dashboards in Python (pandas, NumPy, matplotlib), SQL, and BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, or Datorama).

Experience working with healthcare organizations, regional health authorities, hospitals, integrated care networks, or government health departments is highly desirable.

The successful candidate will have a strong project delivery background in the healthcare sector and ideally experience working as, or in close collaboration with, clinical teams. Applications from clinically trained professionals with project management and healthcare technology experience are welcomed.

Candidates should possess experience in healthcare consulting and/or project management within a healthcare setting, along with an advanced degree in Health Informatics, Health Administration, Business Administration, Computer Science, Health Sciences, or a related field. Experience supporting business development activities, client relationship management, and practice growth initiatives is considered an asset.

ANALYST, ICFR OPERATIONS, INTERNAL AUDIT

Job Description
What is the opportunity?

As an Analyst, ICFR Operations, Internal Audit, you will work in a team, operating in a global resource model that supports the annual SOX and FDICIA testing programs conducted on behalf of management within the Chief Audit Executive group. You will work collaboratively with internal and external stakeholders to conduct testing of key SOX and FDICIA controls within the Bank, including supporting the transformation of strategic initiatives in the program’s delivery. You will also gain exposure to RBC products, services, businesses, and platforms – helping to develop control testing skills, and critical thinking by identifying opportunity for innovative solutions.

RBC Internal Audit leverages a unique perspective— with a coordinated view across the entire business—to deliver valuable advice that not only gives assurance, but that illuminates a bigger picture, connects dots, builds best practices and broadens possibilities. We connect intelligence to protect and enable RBC.

Contribute to the execution of ICFR business and IT control testing, ensuring effective practices are followed and quality documentation is produced by assessing the design and operating effectiveness of key controls.

 Job Posts ML Engineer, Agentic AI Risk & Compliance, GFT

ML ENGINEER, AGENTIC AI RISK & COMPLIANCE, GFT

Job Description
What is the opportunity?

RBC’s AI Innovation team, within Technology & Operations, designs and delivers agentic AI solutions that transform how Risk and Compliance functions operate. We engage stakeholders to identify pain points and build AI-powered solutions – from intelligent document analysis and automated report generation to virtual agents that handle complex, manually intensive tasks. Working alongside subject matter experts, product owners, and engineering teams, we leverage large language models, agentic frameworks, and cloud infrastructure to bring these solutions to production.

This is a roving, high-impact role that will require parachuting into any risk or compliance domain area to deliver AI solutions. It is ideal for someone who is energized by building AI-powered automation and decision-making solutions. The challenges will be a mix of building from scratch and integrating existing systems.

What will you do?

Development: Design, build, and deploy agentic AI systems using Python, large language models, and modern AI frameworks. You will prototype rapidly and iterate solutions into production-grade systems that can interpret natural language queries, retrieve and validate information, automate complex analysis, and triage alerts.

 Job Posts Director, Identity Data And Workflow Engineering (Global Security)

DIRECTOR, IDENTITY DATA AND WORKFLOW ENGINEERING (GLOBAL SECURITY)

Job Description
What is the opportunity?

The Director of Identity Data & Workflow Engineering leads the engineering strategy and delivery of identity data services, workflow automation, and cross-product IAM APIs across the Enterprise Identity and Access Management (EIAM) ecosystem. This role owns the engineering capabilities that power identity request workflows, privileged account inventory, global directory services, and the API layer that enables consistent, secure access to IAM capabilities across the enterprise.

Identity data and workflow engineering is foundational to delivering secure, efficient, and scalable identity services. The quality and accessibility of identity data — and the workflows and APIs that act on it — directly determine how quickly the organization can onboard users, detect risk, and respond to regulatory demands. This leader will build and operate the platforms that turn identity data into a strategic asset and provide a trusted foundation for downstream consumers including controls, reporting, governance, and emerging AI capabilities.

Own the engineering roadmap and delivery for IAM workflow platforms, privileged account inventory, global di

Job description

Genesis Venture Manager, CDL Atlantic

At Genesis, we help build amazing companies. We back founders with the support, connections, and expertise they need to grow.

Genesis, in partnership with Creative Destruction Lab (CDL), is seeking a Venture Manager for a one-year maternity leave replacement position. Based at Genesis in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, this role will support and report to the CDL-Atlantic team (operated out of Dalhousie University). Founded at the University of Toronto's Rotman School of Management, CDL provides startups with access to a powerful network of accomplished entrepreneurs and investors through a recurring nine-month, milestone-based mentoring program focused on developing scalable, science-based startups.

The Genesis Venture Manager will be the CDL representative on the ground in Newfoundland and Labrador. During the execution of the job duties, there will be a focus on deepening the relationships with all stakeholders

description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference. KPMG Forensic helps uncover the facts and create clarity when people and organizations do not agree, or where financial and business behaviour does not comply with expectations. Our professionals can offer support after an incident to help our clients investigate allegations of fraud and misconduct, quantify losses in a commercial dispute, provide expert witness services, and recover damages. This specialized group will give you a unique opportunity to apply and further develop your litigation and forensic and investigative accounting skills. The variety of assignments will provide you with exposure to many interesting and challenging situations. title: Manager, Financial Crimes
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference. At KPMG, we don’t just respond to financial crime, we redefine how the industry is managing the risk. Our newly launched National Financial Crimes Centre of Excellence (FC COE) is a bold step forward in the fight against money laundering, , corruption, and emerging threats. This is your opportunity to be part of a purpose-driven team that combines cutting-edge analytics, global insights, and deep regulatory expertise to protect the financial system and build trust in society. Whether you're a seasoned investigator, a data-driven problem solver, or a strategic thinker passionate about compliance, the FC COE offers a dynamic platform to innovate, collaborate, and lead. We support highly regulated clients in designing, assessing and enhancing governance, risk management and compliance frameworks. This role offers the opportunity to lead complex engagements, develop lasting client relationships and contribute to the evolution of KPMG’s regulatory compliance service offering. Do you want to work in a dynamic and stimulating environment? This opportunity is for you. With the objective of continuing to grow and realize its full potential, our regulatory compliance practice is looking for an experienced Manager or Senior Manager in regulatory compliance to help develop its service offering and grow the team. Based in Quebec City or Montreal, this role will allow you to work with practice partners and multidisciplinary teams to help clients navigate complex regulatory matters and strengthen their compliance programs. In compliance with legislation and sound regulatory risk management practices observed in the industry, our team advises clients on a wide range of regulatory compliance matters, including:governance

title: Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical

Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently seeking a Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical to join our team in Dartmouth, NS.




As a Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician with Kraken Robotics, you will play a key role in the support, definition, and maintenance of Kraken's product portfolios. You will primarily be responsible to produce error-free technical drawings, 3D models, and design verification documentation. You will work within the established workflows of the drawing office, ensuring that all deliverables meet company and industry standards in a timely manner. In this role, you will also play a key role in our Engineering Change Process by executing changes based on approved scopes and that the metadata of inputs and drawings are properly maintained. Key roles and responsibilities include working directly with members of the Drawing Office Team.




Roles and Responsibilities

Mentor a team of Jr. Electrical Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technicians and provide technical “how to”

Association for New Canadians is a non-profit, community-based organization dedicated to the provision of settlement and integration services for immigrants and refugees. 
 
Job Title: Accountant 
Location: St. John’s, NL 
Position Type: 1 full-time position 
Closing date: Until filled 
 
Reporting to the Senior Director of Finance, HR, and IT, the Accountant manages and optimizes financial systems, ensuring data accuracy, integrity, and integration. The role includes generating accurate financial reports, overseeing project and grant accounting, and supporting budgeting and forecasting. 
 
Roles and Responsibilities: 
 1. Financial Reporting and Data Management

Leverage Sage 300’s financial reporting tools to develop customized reports and dashboards that provide insights into program performance, budgets, and funding compliance 
Prepare accurate and timely financial reports, dashboards, and reconciliations 
Analyze financial data, monitor cash flow, and ensure alignment with budgets and funding requirements 


2. Project Accounting and Grants Management

Track income, expenses, and budgets for projects and grants to ensure compliance 
Reconcile financial transactions, review expenditures, and prepare audit-ready documentation 
Support program teams with cash flow analysis and standardized financial management processes

Monitor program budgets, perform variance analysis,

Permanent, Halifax, NS
Competition #48956

Our goal is to build a diverse workforce that reflects the citizens we serve. The Office of the Auditor General has an Employment Equity Policy and welcomes applications from Aboriginal Peoples, African Nova Scotians and Other Racially Visible Persons, Persons with Disabilities, and Women in occupations or positions where they are under-represented. If you belong to one of these groups, we encourage you to self-identify in your cover letter or resume.

Why Join Us?
At the Office of the Auditor General of Nova Scotia, we provide independent assurance to the House of Assembly on the government’s use of public funds and performance. With about 40 professionals, we’re small enough that you’ll quickly build meaningful relationships and have your voice heard, but large enough to give you exposure to complex, high-profile audits that make a real difference in areas like healthcare, education, social services, and public safety. For more information, please visit www.oag-ns.ca.

About Compugen
Compugen is Canada’s largest privately-owned Technology Ally. To innovate industries, transform businesses, connect communities, and drive meaningful change, we must think bigger, reach broader, and act bolder. Through knowledge, curiosity, and collaboration, Compugen helps organizations delivering experience by design. This is what it means to be human-centered and technology-enabled.

Dreaming, designing, and delivering isn’t just a mantra for us — it’s a way of life. We believe that technology is the conduit, but our people – they are the connection that truly makes the magic happen!

Our Culture
We are a human-centered culture where we prioritize your well-being and are invested in helping you reach your fullest potential. We’re not only focused on achieving our goals — we’re dedicated to helping you achieve yours.

If you’re relentlessly curious, driven to make a difference, and collaborative at the core, then you belong with us.

COMPANY DESCRIPTION
Global Spatial Technology Solutions (GSTS) is a Global Maritime Intelligence company delivering solutions to enhance decision-making across the maritime and logistics industry. Our platform, OCIANA, is designed to save lives, energy and the environment on a global scale using innovative data collection and analysis techniques.

GSTS is building a team to support the continued development and commercialization of this capability, which will have a major impact on maritime risk and vessel efficiency globally. We seek individuals with drive, initiative, and motivation to join our team and make the world safer and greener for all.

Are you a software professional that enjoys creating solutions that customers love? Do you thrive on seeing projects from concept to delivery? Do you like working independently in smaller teams and working a solution from beginning to end? Then we have a position for you.

Our development team is growing, and we are looking for a strong developer who has a passion for developing high-quality eLearning products.

As a Software Engineer at Bluedrop, you will be a member of the team responsible for developing and maintaining the Learninglogics suite of web applications, including the Learning Content Management System (LCMS) and Learning Management System (LMS), which are currently being used on many large scale military contracts. You will be involved throughout the product evolution including software development, testing, and in-service support. You will work closely with the rest of the team to build robust applications that are used around the world.

Work with our core development team to plan, design, code, test, and deliver our web-based eLearning products.
Build sophisticated, highly interactive, API driven web applications from the ground up for desktop and mobile using popular frameworks.

 Job Posts Integration Specialist

INTEGRATION SPECIALIST

Bluedrop Training & Simulation Inc. (BTSI) develops advanced training and simulation solutions for the aerospace and defense industry. Our team designs and delivers high-performance virtual and mixed reality training systems used by front-line operators in complex and demanding environments.

As an Integration Specialist, you will play a key role in integrating, configuring, testing, and supporting advanced simulation and training systems throughout the product lifecycle. You will work closely with systems engineering, software development, and production teams to incorporate a variety of third-party hardware and software technologies into our simulation products.

This role combines hands-on technical integration work with customer-facing field support responsibilities and requires occasional travel to customer and test sites.

What you’ll do
Responsibilities

Configure and integrate hardware and software components using vendor-provided documentation and internal procedures
Develop and maintain system setup, configuration, and support documentation
Troubleshoot system-level hardware, software, networking, and integration issues
Perform root cause analysis and develop corrective solutions for identified problems
Collaborate with software, systems, and production teams during integration and product development activities

SENIOR NETWORK SPECIALIST

Sobeys is full of exciting opportunities, and we are always looking for bright new talent to join our team! We currently have a full-time opportunity for a Senior Network Specialist. This role can be based out of our offices located in: Mississauga, ON, Calgary; AB; Stellarton; NS.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead the Network Services team in the implementation, operation, and continuous improvement of a national retail network spanning distribution centers, and corporate locations.
Coordinate day-to-day team activities including prioritization, assignment, and oversight of operational and project-based work.
Design and manage Azure networking including hub-and-spoke architectures, VNets, peering, private endpoints, NSGs, Azure Firewall, and load balancing.
Design, implement, and support scalable network architectures across LAN, WAN, wireless, SD-WAN, and data center environments.
Support private cloud deployments in Azure, enabling secure connectivity between on-prem data centers and cloud environments via VPN and ExpressRoute.
Act as a senior escalation point for complex network and cloud incidents, providing advanced troubleshooting across WAN, SD-WAN, and hybrid cloud environments.
Contribute to multi-cloud networking strategies, ensuring consistent connectivity, routing, and security across Azure and other cloud platforms.

 Job Posts Project Manager

PROJECT MANAGER

The Simulation Product Group focused on the design, fabrication, assembly, integration, delivery and support of mixed-reality (MR) aircraft/helicopter simulators for rear-crew.

The Project Manager role in the Simulation Product Group is responsible for the on-time, on-budget delivery of simulation product projects. In this position you will be working closely with internal and customer stakeholders to identify and coordinate the resolution of issues, risks and barriers that impede project progress and success. You will be also responsible for overseeing project resourcing and cost management in our corporate systems and tools. The position reports to the Senior Director of Systems Engineering.

What you’ll do
Responsibilities:

Measuring and managing project performance (scope, time, cost, risk) using appropriate corporate systems, tools and processes.
Developing and maintaining project schedules in corporate systems.
Performing monthly project financial reconciliation and cost to complete estimations in collaboration with key stakeholders.
Understanding and managing resource allocations across our technical teams in corporate systems; identifying/escalating resources gaps and/or underutilization.
Identifying project risks and issues, and working independently and with stakeholders (internal and external) to mitigate and resolve.

SENIOR FULL STACK DEVELOPER – NODEJS AND REACT

Position Description:
We are growing our capabilities across Atlantic Canada and are seeking a Senior Full Stack Developer with 10+ years of backend development experience to join our team. This is a permanent, full-time role with a hybrid work model requiring 2–3 days per week onsite from one of our Atlantic office locations: Halifax, NS; Moncton, NB; Fredericton, NB; Stratford, PE; or St. John’s, NL.

In this role, you will contribute to the design, development, and optimization of modern web applications, supporting both backend and frontend initiatives. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams, leveraging your experience in Node.js, React, relational databases, API development, cloud services, and DevOps practices to deliver scalable, secure, and high-quality solutions.

Your future duties and responsibilities:

Develop, optimize, and maintain backend services using Node.js
Design and implement RESTful and GraphQL APIs using Apollo Server
Work with relational databases, primarily PostgreSQL (transitioning from MySQL)
Implement caching strategies with Redis to improve performance
Build and deploy applications in AWS using Lambda, ECS, SQS, and RDS
Support CI/CD processes using GitHub Actions and troubleshoot pipeline issues
Integrate backend services with React applications

Introduction
A career in IBM Consulting is built on long-term client relationships and close collaboration worldwide. You’ll work with leading companies across industries, helping them shape their hybrid cloud and AI journeys. With support from our strategic partners, robust IBM technology, and Red Hat, you’ll have the tools to drive meaningful change and accelerate client impact. At IBM Consulting, curiosity fuels success. You’ll be encouraged to challenge the norm, explore new ideas, and create innovative solutions that deliver real results. Our culture of growth and empathy focuses on your long-term career development while valuing your unique skills and experiences.

In this role, you’ll work in one of our IBM Consulting Client Innovation Centers (Delivery Centers), where we deliver deep technical and industry expertise to a wide range of public and private sector clients around the world. Our delivery centers offer our clients locally based skills and technical expertise to drive innovation and adoption of new technology.

Your role and responsibilities

Lead end-to-end implementation and sustainment of SAP Fieldglass and S/4HANA MM solutions
Drive functional design and configuration across service procurement and external workforce management processes
Manage and support myTrack (MCI) contractor onboarding

 Job Posts Donald Hill Family Postdoctoral Fellow – Artificial Intelligence In Health & Medicine

DONALD HILL FAMILY POSTDOCTORAL FELLOW – ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN HEALTH & MEDICINE

Dalhousie University invites applications for a Donald Hill Family Postdoctoral Fellowship in Artificial Intelligence in Health & Medicine. This is an exceptional opportunity for a talented and ambitious researcher to build an independent, high-impact research program at the intersection of machine learning and healthcare.

The fellowship is anchored in the Faculty of Computer Science, under the supervision of Dr. Frank Rudzicz, Dalhousie’s Killam Memorial Chair in AI and Canada CIFAR Chair in AI. The co-supervision team will be assembled around the candidate’s own research vision, drawing from partners across Engineering, Medicine, Health, and Science.

Research Scope
The fellowship is open-ended in research direction, within the area of machine learning applied to health and medicine. We are interested in candidates with expertise and ambition in one or more of the following areas — though we welcome compelling proposals that go beyond them:

Foundation models and large language models in clinical settings (EHR, clinical notes, patient communication)
Speech and language biomarkers for neurological and cognitive disease (dementia, Parkinson’s, depression, psychosis)
Multimodal AI for diagnostics — integrating i

The SOR/APP declaration process as laid out in the data sourcing guideline is a structured and repeatable approach designed to efficiently identify and manage Systems of Record (SORs) and Authorized Provisioning Points (APPs) in accordance with the Enterprise Information Management Risk Policy

Analyze data lineage to identify and validate Systems of Record (SOR) and Authorized Point of Publication (APP) for Critical Data Elements (CDEs).

MICROSOFT FABRIC DATA ENGINEERING SPECIALIST

Position Description:
CGI is seeking a Microsoft Fabric Data Engineer Specialist to support enterprise data modernization initiatives across public sector and commercial clients.

In this role, you will design and implement modern data engineering platforms using Microsoft Fabric, supporting organizations transitioning from traditional Microsoft data platforms to scalable cloud-based analytics architectures.

The successful candidate will have strong hands-on data engineering experience, consulting delivery experience, and the ability to work directly with client stakeholders to deliver production-grade data platforms.

This role is ideal for a senior data engineer with experience modernizing legacy Microsoft data environments such as SQL Server and SSIS into modern Lakehouse architectures.

Data Engineering & Platform Development
Design and implement enterprise data platforms using Microsoft Fabric
Develop Lakehouse architectures using One Lake, Delta tables, and Spark
Build scalable data ingestion and transformation pipelines using Fabric Data Factory
Implement data transformations using PySpark and SQL

Optimize pipelines for performance, scalability, and cost

Junior Electrical Engineer

Junior Electrical Engineer

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

📍 Mount Pearl, NL 

Build technology that operates where few others can—deep ocean environments. 

At Kraken Robotics, we design advanced subsea imaging, battery, and robotic systems used around the world. We’re growing our engineering team and looking for a Junior Electrical Engineer to contribute to the design of mission-critical electronics for next-generation ocean technologies. 




You’ll report to the Engineering Manager, Newfoundland (10+ years in subsea systems engineering). 




What You’ll Work On 

Design of mixed-signal PCBs and embedded electrical systems for subsea robotics and sensing platforms  
Design of wiring harnesses and cable assemblies for electrical systems, considering factors such as signal integrity, thermal management, and EMC/EMI compliance 
Contribute to architecting electrical systems spanning power, signal integrity, and embedded control  
Assist in developing and validating designs for extreme environments (pressure, temperat

title: Director of Admissions
description: Title: Director of Admissions  Location: St. John’s, NL  Drive enrollment growth, conversion performance, and student success.  Venor is proud to partner with our client, Keyin College in their search for a results-driven Director of Admissions to lead student recruitment and enrollment across our growing portfolio of programs.  This senior leadership role will drive admissions performance, enrollment growth, and conversion outcomes through strong team leadership, pipeline management, and operational execution.  About Keyin College  Keyin College is one of Newfoundland and Labrador’s leading post-secondary institutions, with more than 40,000 graduates since 1980. As the province’s largest private college, we provide career-focused education designed to equip learners with practical, in-demand skills that align with workforce needs.  As we continue to grow nationally, we are evolving our admissions function to operate with greater sales rigor, accountability, forecasting discipline, and conversion focus — ensuring we attract, engage, and enroll the right students at scale.  The Role  As Director of Admissions, you will oversee the full admissions funnel, from inquiry to enrollment, ensuring the team consistently delivers strong conversion results and an exceptional prospective student experience.  You will lead a high-performing admissions team in a fast-paced, target-driven environment, fostering a culture of accountability, coaching, responsiveness, and continuous improvemen

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with QuickFacts on their search for a Senior Platform Engineer to join their growing team! QuickFacts is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) company transforming how insurance professionals access and manage information. Their platform brings together underwriting guidelines, carrier documentation, workflows, training resources, and comparison tools into a single, searchable experience that saves clients valuable time and improves decision-making. As QuickFacts continues to expand its AI-powered platform, they are seeking a seasoned backend engineer who thrives on building scalable systems, solving complex technical challenges, and developing innovative solutions that deliver meaningful business impact. This is an opportunity to join a growing technology company where your work will directly influence product strategy, platform architecture, and the future of AI-driven information management within the insurance industry. The Opportunity As a Senior Platform Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing and scaling the services, infrastructure, APIs, and data systems that power QuickFacts' next generation of products. This position sits at the intersection of software engineering, large-scale information systems, and applied AI. You'll work closely with Product, Front-End Engineering, and DevOps teams to transform complex insurance data into intelligent customer experiences. From designing scalable microservices and data pipelines to optimizing AI-

title: Logistics Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with our client, Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd., in the recruitment of a Logistics Coordinator for their Mount Pearl warehouse.     About Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd.  Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd. is Atlantic Canada’s leading industrial rubber supplier, which prides itself on playing a foundational role in the region’s heaviest sectors. We have a dedicated team of experts operating right here in Newfoundland and Labrador. A committed team that is proud to supply robust, high-quality components for a demanding industrial market.  The Opportunity  The Logistics Coordinator will manage the facility's daily inbound and outbound logistics, inventory tracking, and warehouse organization. In this role, you will ensure absolute accuracy and safety during the receiving, storing, packing, and shipping of all goods while maintaining a highly organized and compliant workspace.  Key Responsibilities  Oversee the unloading of goods from trucks. Unpack, inspect, and verify incoming shipments against invoices or purchase orders.  Document any shortages or discrepancies and reject damaged items. Determine the best shipping methods and arrange transport.  Assemble containers/crates, pack, seal, and label outgoing products with precise identifying information and shipping instructions. Prepare and manage bills of lading, customs forms, invoices, and other logistical documents (manually or via computer).  Maintain internal, accurate digital or manual record-keeping system

title: General Manager
description: Venor is proud to partner with Nurse Next Door in their search for a General Manager to lead and grow their Nova Scotia operations. Nurse Next Door is a global home care agency focused on helping people live happier, healthier lives at home and within the communities they call their own. Founded in Vancouver, Nurse Next Door operates through over 400 locally owned and operated locations across Canada, the USA, Australia, and the UK. As one of North America's leading home care providers, Nurse Next Door is redefining how care is delivered by helping seniors remain independent, engaged, and comfortable in their own homes. Built on a philosophy of compassion, innovation, and exceptional customer experiences, the organization has earned a reputation for delivering care that goes beyond tasks and truly makes a difference in people's lives. As General Manager, you will be responsible for the overall success of the Halifax franchise, leading revenue growth, profitability, business development, operations, and team performance. This role combines the responsibilities of a business leader, community ambassador, and people leader, requiring someone who can identify growth opportunities, build strategic partnerships, develop high-performing teams, and deliver exceptional client experiences. Reporting to the Franchise Owner, you will have the opportunity to make a meaningful impact on both business performance and the lives of seniors and their families throughout Nova Scotia.  What You'

title: Warehousing Manager
description: Venor is proud to partner with our client, Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd., in the recruitment of a Warehousing Manager for their Mount Pearl warehouse.     About Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd.  Tamal Industrial Rubber Ltd. is Atlantic Canada’s leading industrial rubber supplier, which prides itself on playing a foundational role in the region’s heaviest sectors. We have a dedicated team of experts operating right here in Newfoundland and Labrador. A committed team that is proud to supply robust, high-quality components for a demanding industrial market.  The Opportunity  The Warehousing Manager will oversee daily logistics operations, coordinate on-the-floor staff, and ensure absolute inventory accuracy across our facility. In this role, you will bridge the gap between upper management and daily operations to ensure the safe, timely, and cost-effective receiving, storing, shipping, and distribution of all materials, parts, and products.  Key Responsibilities  Directing shift schedules, assigning daily tasks, training new hires, and conducting performance reviews.  Overseeing stock takes, tracking shipments, and resolving inventory discrepancies to prevent shortages or overstocking.  Enforcing workplace safety regulations, conducting equipment inspections, and ensuring adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).  Identifying inefficiencies in workflows and implementing process improvements to maximize productivity.  Collaborating with procurement, sales, and logistics teams

title: Consultant, Financial Services-Insurance & Wealth & Asset Management
description: OverviewAt KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference. KPMG’s Management Consulting practice is growing its Strategy & Operations team focused on insurance and wealth management. title: Director of Operations, Dr.Bill
description:
Job Description

RBCx pursues big, bold ideas and leverages RBC’s extensive experience, networks, and capital to help shape what’s next. Our four pillars – Banking, Capital, Platform, and Ventures – combine to support tech businesses of all sizes and stages, making us the go-to backer of Canadian innovation. Our people are our most valuable assets, so we invest deeply in ensuring RBCx isn’t just a place to work but a place to belong.

About Dr.Bill

Dr.Bill is the largest dedicated medical billing provider in Canada, serving 11,000+ physicians and healthcare professionals in Ontario, British Columbia and Alberta. Fully owned by RBC, Dr.Bill is part of the RBCx portfolio. That makes working at Dr.Bill the best of both worlds: our people enjoy the benefits of working for a big company plus the speed and energy of working for a scaling venture.

title: Remote Physical Therapist - Contract
description:
Job Title: Remote Physical TherapistClassification: 1099 Independent ContractorWork Structure: Fully RemoteLocation: United States (AZ, CO, MO, OH, VA, DC, NY, NC, TN, NJ preferred)License Requirements: Active PT license in a PT Compact member state

About BreatheSuite
BreatheSuite delivers virtual pulmonary rehabilitation to patients with chronic lung conditions across the country. Our mission is to make quality pulmonary rehabilitation accessible, empowering patients to take control of their respiratory health from the comfort of their own homes. We use a multidisciplinary model — PTs, respiratory therapists, and nurse practitioners — supported by a remote therapeutic monitoring platform built specifically for this population.

The Role
We're looking for experienced Physical Therapists to join our remote care team as 1099 contractors. You'll be working with a medically complex population — primarily COPD, ILD, and other chronic lung conditions — conducting functional assessments and developing individualized strategies to help patients move better through breathing strategies, activity pacing, strengthening, and cardiovascular exercise. Pulmonary-specific experience is not required. Our compensated onboarding program covers the pathophysiology, symptom management principles, and treatment strategies you need to work confidently with this population before you see your first patient.

What You'll Do

Conduct telehealth evaluations and develop individualized treatment plans

DATA ANALYST

WHO WE ARE
Mariner innovates always and everywhere, turning novel ideas into practical solutions. From products and services to venture creation, we build better futures powered by people.

Our purpose has always been to be a great, everlasting technology company for those who make an impact – elevating people, data, and technology in the best way to solve hard problems.

We partner with people embracing change, leveraging data, cybersecurity, cloud, and change management to drive successful business outcomes that matter to our customers.

Mariners build for change. Like SHIFT Energy, our energy management and decarbonization company. Or East Valley Ventures, created to scale social economic impact in our communities through investment and mentorship of emerging change-makers and their companies.

Mariner’s purpose has always been to be a great, everlasting technology company and the core values that guide us every day are:

OUR VALUES

We care for our team.
We care about the impact we have on our community.
We serve by solving complex problems.
We grow because growth fuels opportunities.
We innovate always and everywhere.
We build a business that lasts.

 Job Posts Senior Facets Solutions Engineer – Claims & Utilization Management

SENIOR FACETS SOLUTIONS ENGINEER – CLAIMS & UTILIZATION MANAGEMENT

About the role
As a Senior Facets Solutions Engineer, you will make an impact by designing and delivering scalable healthcare solutions across claims adjudication, payment integrity, and utilization management. You will be a valued member of our technology and consulting team, collaborating with business stakeholders, project managers, and engineering teams to modernize applications and improve operational efficiency.

This role is ideal for a technology professional with deep Facets expertise and a strong understanding of modern engineering practices, cloud transformation, and healthcare payer systems.

In this role, you will:

Partner with business stakeholders and project teams to identify inefficiencies in current processes and applications, and recommend scalable, outcome‑driven solutions.
Gather, analyze, and clarify business and technical requirements, providing effort estimates and supporting delivery planning.
Design and implement solutions across Facets Claims Adjudication, Payment Integrity, and Utilization Management.
Support application modernization and cloud migration initiatives using modern technologies and best practices.
Apply Agile, DevOps, Automation, and AI concepts to enhance solution delivery and operational efficiency.

 Job Posts Content Marketer

CONTENT MARKETER

COMPANY DESCRIPTION
Global Spatial Technology Solutions (GSTS) is a Global Maritime Intelligence company delivering solutions to enhance decision-making across the maritime and logistics industry. Our platform, OCIANA, is designed to save lives, energy and the environment on a global scale using innovative data collection and analysis techniques.

GSTS is building a team to support the continued development and commercialization of this capability, which will have a major impact on maritime risk and vessel efficiency on a global scale. We seek individuals with drive, initiative, and motivation to join our team and make the world safer and greener for all.

Read more at: www.gsts.ca

ROLE
The Content Marketer position is a strategic, hands-on role responsible for developing compelling content that supports GSTS’s growth objectives across marketing, sales, and brand awareness. The successful candidate will translate complex maritime intelligence and technology concepts into clear, engaging content that resonates with target audiences across the commercial, defence, and public-sector markets.

This role will report to our fractional CMO with a dotted line to our COO, and work closely with the revenue team, product leaders, subject matter experts, and external partners to support demand generation, brand positioning, and thought leadership initiatives

CHIEF OF STAFF

Meet QuickFacts?
QuickFacts is a Software as a Service (SaaS) company that organizes information and presents it on a searchable web-based application. We develop intricate, innovative software that is simple to use and saves our clients hours each day. Currently, we provide the solution to the insurance industry, offering brokerages a one-stop location for organizational workflows as well as all their carrier underwriting information, including custom-made comparisons, documents, training, and helpful tools.

About The Role
We are looking for a highly analytical, systems-driven, and execution-focused Chief of Staff to join our leadership framework. In this role, you will act as the master coordinator of QuickFacts’ internal engine—bridging high-level strategy with day-to-day operational execution.

This is a permanent, high-leverage position designed for a unique operator who balances the structured, framework-driven mindset of a management consultant with the gritty, execution-first mentality of a SaaS builder. Reporting directly to the CEO (and collaborating closely with the Co-Founder/CRO), you will drive company-wide project delivery, protect our corporate compliance, maintain our strategic momentum, and directly manage our People & Culture Coordinator.

Strategic Project Management & Compliance

In today’s tech-driven world, CSDS empowers government departments with essential digital solutions to meet Nova Scotians’ expectations. As a trusted partner across government, we enable departments to fulfill their mandates with secure, user-friendly services. From healthcare and education to public safety, we collaborate to transform how services are designed and delivered.

We don’t just implement technology; we modernize outdated systems and reimagine processes. Our agile, data-driven approach ensures that digital services are both efficient and adaptable to Nova Scotia’s evolving needs.

Your future duties and responsibilities:

The Business Integration Analyst serves as a key liaison between business stakeholders, vendors, and technical teams to design and deliver end-to-end system integrations.

The analyst leads and facilitates workshops to gather, validate, and refine integration requirements, including API and event-based interfaces.
They are responsible for identifying integration touchpoints, documenting data mappings at the field level, defining triggers and error-handling mechanisms, and establishing end-to-end traceability from requirements through testing and release.

Job Description
Posted Monday, June 15, 2026 at 12:00 AM

Engagement & Events Coordinator

 

Bring your expertise to Genoa!

 

Genoa Design International (Genoa) is seeking an Engagement & Events Coordinator to join our Communications & Marketing Team in a full-time, 18 to 24-month contract position at our office in Mount Pearl, NL.

 

ABOUT US

At Genoa, founded in 1995 and a leader in production design and 3D modelling services for the shipbuilding and offshore industries, we believe success is built by people who are passionate, curious, and committed to excellence.

Proudly supporting major initiatives like Canada’s National Shipbuilding Strategy (NSS) and commercial projects across North America, we foster an environment where collaboration drives innovation, learning and development are continuous, and every team member plays a vital role in shaping our future.

The Engagement & Events Coordinator plays a key role in fostering an engaging and inclusive workplace culture. Working closely with the Human Resources, Communications, and Facilities teams, this position coordinates employee engagement initiatives and corporate events that support employee well-being, connection, and organizational culture.

Chef de cuisine

Term Details: 24 months

Job Function: General Services

Location: Embassy of Canada to Jordan

Number of Vacancies: 1

Closing date for application: 26 June, 2026 at 23:59 UTC/GMT +3:00

Apply Here

SUMMARY OF DUTIES

Under the general supervision of the Head of Mission, the Chef de cuisine:

Oversees all culinary aspects of the Embassy of Canada in Jordan, including the planning, creation, and preparation of meals for official and unofficial events (includes formal and informal luncheons, dinners, cocktails and receptions) and the maintenance of the food and alcohol inventories and kitchen supplies. 
Manages the Official Residence (OR) staff and ensure the OR is ready for events. 

 

AREA OF SELECTION

This selection process is open to all applicants, who are residing and legally authorized to work in Jordan, who meet all the essential qualifications and whose applications are received by the closing date.  

Please note that the Embassy of Canada to Jordan does not sponsor work authorizations directly or indirectly.

Join a firm where your career can make a difference. As part of the Federal Government Centre of Excellence, the Marketing & Accounts Specialist plays a key role in executing market-facing initiatives that enhance brand relevance, strengthen client engagement, and support business growth. This role offers hands-on experience across integrated marketing campaigns, account-based marketing, event execution, and data management. While execution-focused, it provides strong exposure to account strategy, Partner priorities, and enterprise initiatives. This is an ideal opportunity for a motivated, versatile marketer who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is eager to build their capabilities across multiple dimensions of marketing and client engagement. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference. Our Risk Services - Governance, Risk and Compliance Services (GRCS) professionals provide a range of risk advisory services to organizations including internal control advisory services, internal audit, enterprise risk management support, assessments of third-party relationships, regulatory compliance, governance reviews and sustainability initiatives.   The successful candidate will play a lead role in executing on the strategic business plan to grow market share, and leading a team of professionals dedicated to servicing internal control over financial reporting (ICFR), initial public offering (IPO) market for key sectors such as Technology, Media and Telecom, Financial Institutions, Industrial Markets, and other high growth sectors in delivering governance, risk and compliance services. We are looking for a dynamic self-starter, with demonstrated leadership capabilities and experience in internal controls over financial reporting, internal audit, enterprise risk and compliance. Their vision & mission is to continuously grow by being the highest value provider to our clients by providing solutions through an outstanding customer experience.  As Logistics Manager, the successful candidate will provide leadership across yard, warehouse, and delivery operations, ensuring systems, processes, and teams are aligned to deliver exceptional service and operational performance. This role is responsible for driving efficiency, inventory accuracy, safety compliance, and employee engagement while fostering a culture of accountability and continuous improvement. Reporting to senior leadership, the Logistics Manager will oversee warehouse and yard operations directly while providing strategic oversight to dispatch and delivery functions. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced logistics leader who thrives in a fast-paced environment and enjoys building high-performing teams. Located in Antigonish, Nova Scotia, The Maples is a 100-unit retirement residence and a vibrant hub within the Mt. Cameron community that offers a wide range of both independent and supportive living arrangements throughout the Antigonish Community. More than a place to live, The Maples offers residents an engaging and active lifestyle through a range of social, wellness, and recreational programming. From fitness classes and golf simulator leagues to pub nights and community events, The Maples is committed to fostering connection, independence, and quality of life for its residents. On their behalf, we are seeking a dynamic and hands-on leader to oversee the day-to-day operations of the residence, including team leadership, financial performance, and the overall resident experience. You will lead a multidisciplinary team, drive operational excellence, and ensure a high standard of hospitality and care across all aspects of the community. This role plays a key part in fostering a vibrant, community-focused environment while balancing resident satisfaction, staff development, and strong business performance. What You’ll be Doing: In this role, you'll translate strategy into action, ensuring The Maples continues to deliver an exceptional living experience for residents while operating efficiently and sustainably. title: Director of Human Resources
location: Moncton OR Fredericton, New Brunswick, CA
description: Are you a high-impact HR leader who believes that a great people strategy is a competitive advantage? FundyPros Specialty Construction is looking for a Director of Human Resources to help build the people infrastructure behind one of Atlantic Canada's most respected specialty contractors. As one of Atlantic Canada's largest specialty contractors, FundyPros Specialty Construction operates across multiple divisions—including Insulation, Firestop, Flooring, and Residential—with revenues in the range of $40–200M. Known for quality workmanship, safety leadership, and commitment to client success, FundyPros is at a pivotal point in its growth. The business is scaling, the talent bar is rising, and this role exists to raise both. The Opportunity Reporting directly to the COO, the Director of Human Resources will serve as a true strategic HR business partner—someone who understands how people programs drive business outcomes and who can translate that understanding into practical, high-impact action. This isn't a transactional HR role. We are looking for someone who balances strategic thinking with execution, bringing structure, accountability, and operational rigor while remaining adaptable in a fast-paced environment. You'll work closely with senior leadership, division managers, and your HR team to align human capital priorities with operational goals. You'll rebuild and elevate core HR functions—talent acquisition, onboarding, learning and development, performance management and

title: Technical Sales Representative
description: Venor is proud to partner with E.H. Price Solutions in their search for a Technical Sales Representative, Air Distribution team to join their team in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia. E.H. Price Solutions is a well-established HVAC solutions provider known for its strong technical expertise, long-standing industry relationships, and customer-focused approach across Atlantic Canada. Working closely with consulting engineers, contractors, and industry partners, the organization has built a strong reputation within the mechanical and air distribution market. Focused specifically within the Air Distribution division, this role is centred around managing and growing established contractors’ relationships across the Atlantic market. The Technical Sales Representative will work closely with mechanical contractors and subcontractors to position and specify E.H. Price Solutions air distribution products on commercial and institutional projects throughout the bid-specification process. What You’ll Be Doing In this role, you will act as a trusted technical sales advisor to engineers, contractors, and industry stakeholders while supporting specification-driven sales growth across the region. This replacement role, under some direction, plans, directs, and coordinates activities of medium to high level of complexity product initiatives to ensure that they meet the approved timeframe, scope and quality, expectations. title: Remote Registered Respiratory Therapist
description:
Job Title: Remote Registered Respiratory Therapist
Classification: Part TimeWork Structure: Fully RemoteLocation: Anywhere in US License Requirements: Active RT license in Colorado, Arizona, Utah, or Ohio 
About BreatheSuite
BreatheSuite is transforming pulmonary rehabilitation through virtual care, providing accessible, high-quality respiratory therapy for patients with chronic lung conditions. Our mission is to empower individuals to take control of their respiratory health through personalized, evidence-based care—all from the comfort of their homes.
What We’re Looking For
We are seeking experienced Registered Respiratory Therapists (RRTs) to join our virtual care team. This role offers flexibility while allowing you to make a meaningful impact by delivering patient-centered care in a telehealth setting.
Responsibilities:

Provide Virtual Care: Conduct telehealth visits to assess patients, develop care plans, and provide ongoing respiratory therapy for individuals with chronic lung conditions.
Patient Education & Coaching: Educate patients on inhaler techniques, airway clearance strategies, breathing exercises, and self-management techniques as well as providing chronic disease management education.
Collaborate Across Disciplines: Work closely with physical therapists and nurse practitioners to ensure a multidisciplinary approach to care.
Accurate Documentation: Complete timely and thorough documentation using BreatheSuite’s EMR and Care Team Dashboard.

Qualifications

Our Approach

Stewart McKelvey has a long history of being a leader in Atlantic Canada. And being a leader means embracing innovation.

We changed the traditional approach to law student recruitment in Atlantic Canada. Typically, Articled Clerks wait for “hire-back” decisions well into their articling year.

Stewart McKelvey is thinking differently.

We believe that success in the practice of law requires making a commitment for more than one summer or one year. Our innovative approach removes the traditional “hire-back” decision-making process, typically done at the end of the articling year. A 2L Summer Student will advance to becoming an Articled Clerk, then directly to an Associate at Stewart McKelvey.

This allows Articled Clerks to focus on key competency development – strong legal analysis, developing a substantive knowledge base, writing, communication, and time management – all the skills required to develop the region’s best lawyers, on the pat

title: Senior Full Stack Mobile Developer - Mydoh
description:
Job Description

Mydoh is an award-winning Toronto-based fintech committed to helping Canadian families raise money-smart kids. As part of RBC Ventures, Mydoh is seeking to change the financial landscape for families. Through real-world money experiences, Mydoh makes it easier for the next generation to develop core financial habits to set young people up for success. At Mydoh, we understand the importance of financial literacy and believe that developing good money habits should start early – our product gives youth the opportunity to learn money management first-hand through (earn, save, spend) all with parental oversight and coaching.
Our Purpose? Simply put, to help the next generation thrive.

What will you do?

  • Work with Typescript, NestJS, and PostgreSQL to create, test, and implement code for our users' needs making exciting new features for families using Mydoh in Canada.

  • Maintain code quality and best practices across mobile and backend with an eye for security standards

Marine Atlantic is committed to a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. Preference may be given to members of the following equity deserving groups: women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities, visible minorities and 2SLGBTQIA+ community members.  If you are a member of one or more of these groups, please state to which group(s) you identify in your application.
